alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study represents a major contribution in the prediction of displacement and strain fields based solely on the greyscale content of undeformed XCT images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study represents a major contribution in the prediction of displacement and strain fields based solely on the greyscale content of undeformed XCT images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future development of D 2 IM will aim at clinical translation for improved diagnostics by incorporating a wider range of structures and loading scenarios. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of D2IM to lower resolution imaging in initial testing provides promising results, indicating the future viability of integrating this technology into a clinical setting. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides initial promising results (lower-resolution testing) that make the projection toward clinical integration more grounded, increasing confidence in future clinical viability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep learning framework achieved an average precision of >90% in detection of micronuclei. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep learning framework achieved an average precision of >90% in detection of micronuclei. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact performance claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This proof of principle investigation supports the idea of deploying AI powered tools in a cost-effective manner for repetitive and laborious tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study supports the idea of deploying AI powered tools in a cost-effective manner for repetitive and laborious tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about cost-effective deployment for repetitive tasks is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of AI powered tools will help improve the quality of data and wellbeing of researchers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of AI powered tools will help improve the quality of data and wellbeing of researchers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about data quality and researcher wellbeing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of pre-trained deep convolutional neural networks ResNet50, InceptionV3, and InceptionResnetV2 achieves higher predictive accuracy than previously reported, between 95 and 97%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The networks achieved higher predictive accuracy than previously reported, with accuracy rates between 95% and 97%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical accuracy claim and comparison to prior work are reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of transfer learning and deep convolutional neural networks allows for quick and accurate distinction between different cell morphologies from a limited amount of labeled data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates the combined benefit of transfer learning and deep convolutional neural networks for distinguishing between different cell morphologies from limited labeled data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey the same conclusion about transfer learning enabling accurate morphology distinction from limited labels.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks provide a compelling alternative to traditional image analysis workflows for quantifying changes in cell morphology. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of pretrained deep convolutional neural networks ResNet50, InceptionV3, and InceptionResnetV2 allows for the prediction of cell mechanisms of action in response to chemical perturbations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes a specific application (predicting mechanisms of action) rather than the broader methodological claim of being an alternative workflow; the contribution is reframed more concretely, a clarification with somewhat different emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning can derive measures of mental health from brain signals and questionnaire data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Population modeling with machine learning can derive measures of mental health from heterogeneous inputs, including brain signals and questionnaire data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core claim that ML can derive mental-health measures from brain signals and questionnaires, framing it in population-modeling terms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Proxy measures built using machine learning were as useful, and sometimes more useful, than original measures for characterizing real-world health behavior. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proxy measures derived from machine learning are as useful, and sometimes more useful, than original neuropsychological measures for characterizing real-world health behavior. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative utility claim for proxy measures.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sociodemographic information contributed most to characterizing psychological traits beyond aging. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning applied to multimodal MR images and sociodemographic information can build proxy measures of psychological constructs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms that sociodemographic data are part of multimodal inputs but does not support the stronger preprint claim that sociodemographics contributed most; this represents a toned-down, narrowed claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning can define empirical approximations of health-related psychological measures that do not require human judgment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning applied to multimodal MR images and sociodemographic information can build proxy measures of psychological constructs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the general contribution claim about building proxy measures but omits the explicit assertion that these do not require human judgment, thus softening the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Participants exhibited reduced task-switching costs after simulated psychedelic exposure compared to naturalistic exposure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants exhibited reduced task-switching costs after simulated psychedelic exposure compared to naturalistic exposure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are effectively identical in wording and implication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No significant differences were observed between naturalistic and simulated psychedelic exposure in linguistic association tasks at word and sentence levels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: No significant differences were observed between naturalistic and simulated psychedelic exposure in linguistic automatic association tasks at word and sentence levels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the adjective "automatic," slightly clarifying the task type while preserving the original result and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Visually grounded high-level cognitive processes were modulated by exposure to simulated hallucinations, as evidenced by participants drawing performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Visually grounded high-level cognitive processes were modulated by exposure to simulated hallucinations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result but omits the specific evidence detail (drawings), making the claim more concise without changing its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Altering perceptual phenomenology through simulated visual hallucinations significantly modulates high-level human cognition. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results provide insights into the interdependence of bottom-up and top-down cognitive processes and altered states of consciousness without pharmacological intervention. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's strong causal claim about modulation of high-level cognition is reframed in the publication as more interpretive insights into cognitive interdependence and altered states, with lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research may offer valuable insights into altered states of consciousness without pharmacological intervention. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results provide insights into the interdependence of bottom-up and top-down cognitive processes and altered states of consciousness without pharmacological intervention. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution wording about offering insights into altered states without pharmacology; the match is direct and retains the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method, OpenMM-Loss, improves the structural quality and reduces potential energy of predicted protein structures when applied to OpenFold. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Subsequently predicted protein structures exhibit comparable accuracy while displaying lower potential energy and improved structural quality as assessed by MolProbity metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same finding with additional detail on assessment metrics (MolProbity) and mention of comparable accuracy; substance is consistent though slightly more specific.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OpenMM-Loss can be applied to any all-atom representation of protein structure that can map into the software package, SidechainNet. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: OpenMM-Loss can be applied to any all-atom representation of a protein structure capable of mapping into the software package, SidechainNet. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same applicability claim but with higher certainty (medium -> high), effectively amplifying confidence in the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The incorporation of molecular dynamics forces as gradients for training deep learning models like AlphaFold2 may enhance their ability to recapitulate fundamental biophysical principles. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new method to train deep learning protein structure prediction models using molecular dynamics force fields could improve their functionality. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the conceptual conclusion more generally as potential improvement in functionality; the meaning is preserved but phrased differently.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predicted protein structures after applying the relaxation procedure exhibit comparable accuracy to those predicted before the procedure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Subsequently predicted protein structures exhibit comparable accuracy while displaying lower potential energy and improved structural quality as assessed by MolProbity metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert comparable accuracy after the procedure; the publication additionally highlights lowered energy and improved quality but does not contradict the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepProLigand predicts protein-ligand interactions from cryo-EM density maps of proteins and ligands. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepProLigand is a deep learning bioinformatics pipeline developed to predict protein-ligand interactions from cryo-EM density maps. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication and preprint describe the same capability; wording differences reflect contribution/result framing but the content is consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method was ranked first in fitting ligands to cryo-EM density maps in the 2021 EMDataResource Ligand Challenge. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method was ranked first in fitting ligands to cryo-EM density maps in the 2021 EMDataResource Ligand Challenge. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical reporting of the challenge ranking across preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning bioinformatics approach is a promising direction to model protein-ligand interaction on cryo-EM data using prior structural information. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning bioinformatics approach demonstrates promise for modeling protein-ligand interactions using cryo-EM data and prior structural information. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same cautious optimism about the approach; wording differences are minor and do not change meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both deep-learning approaches outperformed an existing baseline automated approach and performed similarly to two independent experts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both deep-learning approaches outperformed an existing baseline automated approach and performed similarly to two independent experts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the semi-supervised approach was superior and implemented into AxonDeep. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the semisupervised approach was superior and implemented into AxonDeep. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match in content and certainty; wording difference negligible.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AxonDeep performs automated quantification and segmentation of axons from healthy appearing nerves and those with mild to moderate degrees of damage. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AxonDeep performs automated quantification and segmentation of axons from healthy-appearing nerves and those with mild to moderate degrees of damage. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same conclusion repeated in the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Use of deep learning for axon quantification provides rapid, objective, and higher throughput analysis of optic nerve. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Use of deep learning for axon quantification provides rapid, objective, and higher throughput analysis of optic nerve. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same evaluative conclusion about deep learning benefits.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine-human consistency was significantly lower than inter-curator (humanhuman) consistency. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine-human consistency in phenotype annotations was significantly lower than inter-curator (human-human) consistency. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same statistically significant result about machine-human vs inter-curator consistency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Allowing curators access to external information did not significantly increase the similarity of their annotations to the Gold Standard. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Allowing curators access to external information did not significantly increase the similarity of their annotations to the gold standard. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match in content and certainty between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The similarity of machine annotations to the Gold Standard increased after new ontology terms relevant to the input text had been added. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The similarity of machine annotations to the gold standard increased after new relevant ontology terms had been added. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same result conveyed with minor wording adjustments; meaning and certainty preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gold Standard annotations came closer to representing the intended meaning of character descriptions than did either the curator or machine annotations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gold standard annotations better represented the intended meaning of character descriptions than either curator or machine annotations, according to evaluation by the original authors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds that this evaluation was performed by the original authors, clarifying the basis of the comparison while preserving the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Gold Standard corpus will allow training and assessment of new tools to improve phenotype annotation accuracy at scale. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the forward-looking claim about the corpus enabling training and assessment at scale; instead it focuses on dataset development and evaluation, so the specific future-utility claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study generated two machine learning models aimed to identify myeloid malignant transcriptional profiles in scRNAseq datasets from the bone marrow of patients with FA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep neural network model was developed to predict the presence of AML-like cells in scRNA-seq datasets from bone marrow samples of patients with FA.
alignments.rationale: Both describe model development for detecting malignant myeloid-like transcriptional profiles, but the publication narrows to a single deep neural network and emphasizes AML-like cells rather than two models targeting myeloid malignancy generally.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both predictors displayed high sensitivity, specificity, and accuracy for detection of single cell resolution myeloid malignant transcriptional profiles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictor displayed high sensitivity, specificity, and accuracy for the detection of single-cell resolution myeloid malignant transcriptional profiles.
alignments.rationale: The result about high sensitivity/specificity/accuracy at single-cell resolution is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predicted MDS and AML cells from FA patients are enriched in the lympho-myeloid-primed progenitor (LMPP) and the granulocyte-monocyte progenitor (GMP) populations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Functional analyses of the predicted-AML cells from FA patients showed enrichment of lympho-myeloid-primed progenitor and granulocyte-monocyte progenitor populations.
alignments.rationale: Both describe enrichment of LMPP and GMP among predicted malignant (AML/MDS) cells; the publication frames it as functional analysis of predicted-AML cells but the biological enrichment claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predicted MDS and AML cells have gene expression and master transcriptional factor profiles that suggest malignant transformation and that differ from the rest of FA cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transcriptional profiles associated with malignant transformation were identified in the predicted-AML cells from FA patients.
alignments.rationale: Both report transcriptional signatures of malignant transformation, but the publication focuses on predicted-AML cells (not explicitly MDS+AML) and omits the explicit mention of master TF profiles, indicating a narrowed/revised presentation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed model forecasts future SARS-CoV-2 mutation surges in real-time with low prediction error confined within 0.1% and 1% for predictions made for the next 30 and 80 days respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed model forecasts future SARS-CoV-2 mutation surges in real-time with low prediction error confined within 0.1% and 1% for the next 30 and 80 days, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numerical real-time forecasting performance for 30- and 80-day horizons.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method led to a notable increase in prediction accuracy compared to traditional time-series models, as evidenced by lower MAE and MSE for predictions made within different time horizons.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method led to a notable increase in prediction accuracy compared to traditional time-series models, as evidenced by much lower MAE and MSE.
alignments.rationale: The core comparative result is preserved, but the publication emphasizes a stronger improvement ("much lower" MAE/MSE), hence an amplification of confidence in the superiority.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrated its robustness by accurately predicting unseen mutation patterns when training on data from two pattern categories while testing on the third pattern category.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrated its robustness by accurately predicting unseen mutation patterns when trained on data from two pattern categories while testing on the third.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication describe the same cross-pattern generalization experiment and conclude robustness in predicting unseen patterns.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology has potential applicability in studying other infectious diseases, thus extending its relevance beyond the current pandemic.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodology has potential applicability to study other infectious diseases or forecasting tasks beyond the current COVID pandemic.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim of broader applicability but explicitly extends it to other forecasting tasks as well, broadening the stated scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Variable Convolutional (vConv) layer demonstrates superior performance in motif identification compared to traditional convolutional layers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed variable convolutional (vConv) layer demonstrates superior performance in motif identification compared to traditional convolutional layers.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance superiority claim for vConv in motif identification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: vConv learns kernel length from data adaptively, enhancing its effectiveness in high-throughput omics data analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: vConv layers learn kernel length from data adaptively, enhancing their effectiveness in high-throughput omics data analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same methodological claim but with slightly reduced certainty/labeling (medium vs high), so the claim is clarified with a tempered confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: vConv can be integrated into multi-layer neural networks as an in-place replacement for canonical convolutional layers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: vConv can be integrated into multi-layer neural networks as an 'in-place replacement' for canonical convolutional layers.
alignments.rationale: Both describe vConv as a drop-in/in-place replacement for canonical conv layers; the claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BenchXAI is a novel XAI benchmarking package that supports comprehensive evaluation of fifteen XAI methods in biomedical data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BenchXAI is a novel XAI benchmarking package that supports comprehensive evaluation of fifteen XAI methods in biomedical data.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim about BenchXAI and its support for evaluating fifteen XAI methods is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The XAI methods Integrated Gradients, DeepLift, DeepLiftShap, and GradientShap performed well across three common biomedical tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The XAI methods Integrated Gradients, DeepLift, DeepLiftShap, and GradientShap performed well over all three biomedical tasks evaluated.
alignments.rationale: The performance ranking/observation for these XAI methods is preserved and described consistently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Methods like Deconvolution, Guided Backpropagation, and LRP-α 1-β 0 struggled in some tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Methods like Deconvolution, Guided Backpropagation, and LRP-α1-β0 struggled for some tasks in the evaluation.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same observation but with a higher certainty label (high), indicating amplified confidence in the negative performance finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of XAI in the biomedical domain is becoming increasingly essential due to regulations like the EU AI Act.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of XAI in the biomedical domain is becoming more essential due to regulations like the EU AI Act.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication make the same normative observation about regulatory drivers (EU AI Act) increasing the importance of XAI in biomedicine.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A host-specific technique has been developed for annotating interferon-gamma (IFN-γ) inducing peptides, which is an updated version of IFNepitope.
alignments.pub_claim_text: IFNepitope2 is an updated technique for annotating IFN-γ inducing peptides that outperforms existing methods on an independent dataset.
alignments.rationale: Both claim an updated IFNepitope technique; the publication additionally frames it as outperforming existing methods, so the contribution is clarified/expanded in the published text.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best machine learning based models achieved AUROC 0.89 and 0.83 for human and mouse host, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models based on extra tree perform better than other machine learning techniques for predicting IFN-γ inducing peptides.
alignments.rationale: The preprint reported specific AUROC values for best ML models; the publication emphasizes that extra tree models perform best without reporting the same numeric split, changing the framing of the performance claim and lowering reported certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A hybrid model combining the best machine learning based model with BLAST achieved AUROC 0.90 and 0.85 for human and mouse host, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hybrid model developed in this study achieved an AUROC of 0.90 for human and 0.85 for mouse host.
alignments.rationale: The hybrid model AUROC values are preserved exactly between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The newly developed method performs better than existing methods on an independent dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: IFNepitope2 is an updated technique for annotating IFN-γ inducing peptides that outperforms existing methods on an independent dataset.
alignments.rationale: Both state that the updated method outperforms existing methods on an independent dataset; this conclusion is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The IFNepitope2 server has been developed to predict, design, and scan IFN-γ inducing peptides.
alignments.pub_claim_text: IFNepitope2 is an updated technique for annotating IFN-γ inducing peptides that outperforms existing methods on an independent dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication names IFNepitope2 as the updated technique (implying the tool/service) though it emphasizes performance; the server claim is preserved in spirit but the publication frames it more as a method/outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A set of 5 genes (ITGA10, CXCR4, ADCYAP1, SLC6A12, VGF) were identified following differential gene expression and feature importance analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The feature selection process led to the identification of a compact set of five impactful genes for Alzheimer's identification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the identification of a compact five-gene signature; the publication restates this result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The random forest algorithm with customized hyperparameters was found to be the best-performing model for differentiating Alzheimer’s disease samples from control. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ensemble model consisting of logistic regression, naive Bayes classifier, and support vector machine was identified as the best performing model for AITeQ. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert a best-performing model for classification, but the specific algorithm changed from random forest to an optimized ensemble in the publication, so the claim was revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AITeQ, a user-friendly, reliable, and accurate machine learning framework for Alzheimer’s disease prediction was developed based on the 5 gene signature. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Alzheimer's Identification Tool (AITeQ) was developed using RNA-seq data and a machine learning model based on an optimized ensemble algorithm. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the AITeQ contribution but clarifies implementation details (RNA-seq data and an optimized ensemble) relative to the preprint's description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MethylNet can study cellular differences and grasp higher order information of cancer sub-types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MethylNet can study cellular differences and grasp higher order information of cancer sub-types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint claim verbatim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MethylNet can estimate age and capture factors associated with smoking in concordance with known differences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MethylNet can estimate age and capture factors associated with smoking in concordance with known differences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ability of MethylNet to capture nonlinear interactions presents an opportunity for further study of unknown disease, cellular heterogeneity and aging processes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ability of MethylNet to capture nonlinear interactions presents an opportunity for further study of unknown disease, cellular heterogeneity and aging processes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion appears unchanged in text and emphasis between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Label-free imaging flow cytometry and deep learning can characterize RBC morphologies during 42-day storage. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Label-free imaging flow cytometry and deep learning can effectively characterize RBC lesions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims state that the combined approach can characterize RBC morphology/lesions; the publication focuses language on lesions/effectiveness rather than the storage-day framing, a mild clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model achieved 90% accuracy in classifying six different RBC morphologies associated with storage lesions versus human-curated manual examination. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A trained neural network achieved 76.7% agreement with experts in classifying RBC morphologies associated with storage lesions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims report agreement with human experts on morphology classification, but the reported performance dropped from 90% (preprint) to 76.7% agreement in the publication, so the numeric result was revised and confidence tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A model fitted to the deep learning-extracted features revealed a pattern of morphological changes within the aging blood unit that allowed predicting the expiration date of stored blood. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The feature space extracted by the weakly supervised neural network better predicted blood quality than the conventional expert-assessed morphology classification system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint claimed prediction of expiration date specifically; the publication reframes results as improved prediction of blood quality relative to experts, which narrows the original expiration-date claim and reports a different emphasis and metric.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning and label-free imaging flow cytometry could reduce complex laboratory procedures and facilitate robust and objective characterization of blood samples. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning and label-free imaging flow cytometry could automate the assessment of RBC storage lesions, minimizing laboratory sample handling and reducing procedural errors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution in more concrete operational terms (automation, reduced handling/errors) but the overall contribution claim is consistent with the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A specialized deep learning model can identify functional enhancer mutations in phased melanoma genomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Training the DeepMEL2 model on melanoma chromatin accessibility data captures various regulatory programs of melanocytic and mesenchymal-like melanoma cell states. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes that model training captures regulatory programs relevant to melanoma states, which clarifies the model's scope and basis relative to the preprint's statement about identifying enhancer mutations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model predicts and interprets the impact of mutations on gene regulation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DeepMEL2 model outperforms motif-based variant scoring and more generic deep learning models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the model's predictive/interpretive ability in comparative performance terms (outperforming other scoring methods), which clarifies and provides evidence for the preprint's claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Identified mutations are associated with allelic imbalance of chromatin accessibility and gene expression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fifteen to twenty percent of allele-specific chromatin accessibility variants can be explained by gains or losses of transcription factor binding sites. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims link variants to allele-specific chromatin accessibility and regulatory mechanisms; the publication quantifies the explanatory fraction, thereby clarifying the preprint's association.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model D-GPM outperforms linear regression (LR) by 9.59% in mean absolute deviation (MAE) and 4.34% in Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: D-GPM outperforms regression tree, linear regression, and support vector machine in 95.66%, 92.65%, and 85.49% of the target genes based on its relatively lower MAE. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a different form of performance evidence (percent of genes with lower MAE) rather than the preprint's percent-improvement metrics, representing a substantive revision and broader supporting evidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: D-GPM outperforms regression tree (RT) by 27.58% in MAE and 22.96% in PCC. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: D-GPM outperforms regression tree, linear regression, and support vector machine in 95.66%, 92.65%, and 85.49% of the target genes based on its relatively lower MAE. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the specific percent-improvement figures with gene-wise outperforming frequencies; this revises the numeric comparison while providing stronger, broader evidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NetSurfP-2.0 predicts solvent accessibility with a correlation of 80% between predictions and experimental data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: NetSurfP-2.0 achieves a correlation of 80% between predictions and experimental data for solvent accessibility.
alignments.rationale: Numeric performance claim is restated unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NetSurfP-2.0 achieves a precision of 85% on secondary structure 3-class predictions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: NetSurfP-2.0 demonstrates a precision of 85% on secondary structure 3-class predictions.
alignments.rationale: Performance metric is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The processing time of NetSurfP-2.0 has been optimized to allow predicting more than 1,000 proteins in less than 2 hours.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The processing time of NetSurfP-2.0 has been optimized to allow predicting more than 1000 proteins in less than 2 hours.
alignments.rationale: Runtime/throughput claim is the same, differing only in minor formatting of the number.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Principal component analysis in advance of elastic net regression led to the overall best performing DNA methylation-based telomere length estimator.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Principal component analysis in advance of elastic net regression led to the overall best performing DNA methylation-based telomere length estimator.
alignments.rationale: The core methodological result is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The baseline model of elastic net regression with no prior feature reduction stage performed worst, suggesting a prior feature-selection stage may have important utility.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The baseline model of elastic net regression with no prior feature reduction stage performed less well, suggesting a prior feature-selection stage may have important utility.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the conclusion about baseline model performance and the utility of prior feature selection, with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Estimators are sensitive to data set heterogeneity, indicating that the development of an optimal DNA methylation-based estimator should benefit from a robust methodological approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The development of an optimal DNA methylation-based estimator should benefit from the robust methodological approach used in this study.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses the takeaway on benefiting from the presented methodological approach rather than explicitly stating estimator sensitivity to heterogeneity; this is a clarification of emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different DNA methylation-based telomere length estimators, which have few common CpGs, are associated with many of the same biological entities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Different DNA methylation-based telomere length estimators, which have few common CpGs, are associated with many of the same biological entities.
alignments.rationale: The association result is reproduced verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology utilized in this study could be applied to other biological markers and disease phenotypes to examine their relationship with DNA methylation and predictive value.
alignments.rationale: This forward-looking application claim in the preprint does not appear in the publication's listed claims, indicating it was removed or not carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed software, RBPSpot, achieved an average accuracy of approximately 90% across a large number of validated datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed software, RBPSpot, achieved an average accuracy of approximately 90% across a large number of validated datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative performance claim verbatim; no change in scope or confidence is apparent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RBPSpot outperformed recently developed tools, including those with more complex deep-learning models, during a comprehensive benchmarking process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RBPSpot outperformed other tools, including those with more complex deep-learning models, during a comprehensive benchmarking process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the benchmarking result with near-identical meaning (minor wording changes do not alter the claim).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Contextual features based on pentamers/dinucleotides, which capture shape and structure properties, are critical for evaluating RBP binding potential. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No equivalent statement about pentamer/dinucleotide contextual features appears in the publication; the feature-specific conclusion was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RBPSpot can be used to build new models for any species and any RBP using user-provided data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RBPSpot can identify RBP binding sites in the human system and can be used to develop new models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the model-building capability but narrows the claimed applicability to the human system rather than 'any species and any RBP', representing a narrowing of scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our real-valued distance prediction obtains 81% precision on top L/5 long-range contact prediction, much better than the best CASP13 results (70%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our real-valued distance prediction obtains 81% precision on top L/5 long-range contact prediction, much better than the best CASP13 results (70%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this quantitative performance claim exactly, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our real-valued prediction can predict correct folds for the same number of CASP13 FM targets as the best CASP13 group, despite generating only 20 decoys for each target. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our real-valued prediction can predict correct folds for the same number of CASP13 FM targets as the best CASP13 group, despite generating only 20 decoys for each target. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains this claim verbatim; no change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method greatly outperforms a very new real-valued prediction method DeepDist in both contact prediction and 3D structure modeling. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method greatly outperforms a very new real-valued prediction method DeepDist in both contact prediction and 3D structure modeling. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparative superiority to DeepDist is retained exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our real-valued distance prediction has 1-6% higher contact and distance accuracy than our own discrete-valued prediction, but less accurate 3D structure models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our real-valued distance prediction has 1-6% higher contact and distance accuracy than our own discrete-valued prediction, but less accurate 3D structure models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The nuanced comparison between real-valued and discrete-valued predictions is retained without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BABEL is a deep learning method that translates between the transcriptome and chromatin profiles of a single cell. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BABEL is a deep learning method that translates between the transcriptome and chromatin profiles of a single cell. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the method/contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BABEL can generate scRNA-seq directly from a cell’s scATAC-seq, and vice versa. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BABEL accurately translates between single-cell ATAC and gene expression modalities for individual cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the modality-translation capability (generate expression from ATAC and vice versa) as accurate translation for individual cells; meaning is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BABEL accurately translates between scRNA-seq and scATAC-seq modalities for individual cells across several datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BABEL accurately translates between single-cell ATAC and gene expression modalities for individual cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms accurate single-cell translation; dataset generalization phrasing is condensed but the core accuracy claim remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BABEL can incorporate additional single-cell data modalities, such as CITE-seq, enabling translation across chromatin, RNA, and protein. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BABEL can incorporate additional single-cell data modalities, such as protein epitope profiling, enabling translation across chromatin, RNA, and protein. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the multimodal extensibility claim but changes the exemplar modality (CITE-seq → protein epitope profiling), a clarification of examples rather than a substantive scope change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BABEL generated scRNA-seq from scATAC-seq of patient derived basal cell carcinoma that enabled fine-grained classification of complex cell states. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BABEL can generate single-cell expression from scATAC-seq data of patient-derived basal cell carcinoma, enabling fine-grained classification of complex cell states. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this specific application result with equivalent wording and implications.

alignments.pub_claim_text: BABEL generalizes well to new biological contexts not seen during training. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an explicit generalization claim that does not appear in the preprint; this appears to be an added high-level conclusion based on further analysis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of image-based real-time fluorescence and deformability cytometry with standing surface acoustic waves enables the separation of cell mimics and blood cell types based on fluorescence and deformability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of real-time fluorescence and deformability cytometry with sorting based on standing surface acoustic waves enhances molecular specificity in cell sorting. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the experimental separation result as an enhancement of molecular specificity; the core contribution is preserved but emphasized differently.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identification of blood sub-populations is enhanced by flow alignment and deformation of cells in the microfluidic channel constriction. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of real-time fluorescence and deformability cytometry with sorting based on standing surface acoustic waves enhances molecular specificity in cell sorting. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication broadens and strengthens the microfluidic/flow-based enhancement claim into a general statement about improved molecular specificity, implying stronger contribution-level framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep neural network trained on hundreds of thousands of labeled cell images can identify and sort unlabeled blood cells at rates of 100 cells/sec. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This method successfully sorts neutrophils from whole blood without the use of labels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a concrete application (label-free neutrophil sorting) rather than the broader, high-throughput claim about generic unlabeled blood cell sorting at a stated rate, indicating a narrower reported result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach transfers molecular specificity into label-free sorting, opening up new possibilities for biological research and clinical applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An efficient deep neural network is used to transfer molecular specificity to image-based sorting. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same conceptual contribution (transferring molecular specificity via a DNN) and presents it with stronger framing and emphasis on efficiency, increasing the contribution-level confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Current label-free cell sorting approaches lack molecular specificity and speed. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states a limitation of existing methods that was not present as a standalone claim in the preprint, so this is an added contextual claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained deep learning architecture, DanQ, can predict chromatin accessibility from leaf ATAC-seq data across 12 angiosperm species. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The across-species models have comparable or even superior performance to a model trained within species, suggesting strong conservation of chromatin mechanisms across angiosperms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms cross-species predictive performance consistent with the preprint's multi-species prediction claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models trained across species demonstrate comparable or superior performance to those trained within species, indicating strong conservation of chromatin mechanisms across angiosperms. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The across-species models have comparable or even superior performance to a model trained within species, suggesting strong conservation of chromatin mechanisms across angiosperms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the cross-species performance and the inferred conservation conclusion with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models are most effective at predicting constitutively-accessible chromatin regions, with performance decreasing as cell-type specificity increases. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our models are best at predicting constitutively accessible chromatin regions, with diminishing performance as cell-type specificity increases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result, and presents it with stronger (higher) confidence framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TCP and AP2/ERF transcription factor families consistently rank highly in importance across the models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TCP and AP2/ERF transcription factor families consistently ranked highly in importance to each model. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the TF-family importance finding with equivalent meaning.

alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepMF achieved the highest clustering accuracy of 76%, 100%, 92%, and 100% for medulloblastoma cancer, leukemia cancer, breast cancer, and small-blue-round-cell cancer respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When analyzing data sets with 70% missing entries, DeepMF outperformed other state-of-the-art matrix factorization tools on cancer data sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: When analyzing data sets with 70% missing entries, DeepMF outperformed other state-of-the-art matrix factorization tools on cancer data sets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepMF demonstrated robust denoising, imputation, and embedding ability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The gut virome exhibits a higher degree of temporal stability than previously observed in persistent personal viromes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IPEV is a high-performance, user-friendly tool that assists biologists in identifying and classifying prokaryotic and eukaryotic viruses within viromes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: IPEV is a novel method that distinguishes prokaryotic and eukaryotic viruses in viromes using a 2-dimensional convolutional neural network. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SC-Track performs better than a panel of publicly available cell trackers on a diverse set of cell segmentation types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SC-Track performs better than a panel of publicly available cell trackers on a diverse set of cell segmentation types. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: SC-Track maintains robust cell-tracking performance in varying cell segmentation qualities, cell morphological appearances, and imaging conditions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Current CNNs are prone to produce noisy cell segmentations and classifications, which is a significant barrier to constructing accurate single-cell lineages. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained ANN demonstrated striking similarities in computation and heat representation to a known zebrafish BNN. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The designed and trained ANN for heat gradient navigation exhibits striking similarities in computation and heat representation to a known zebrafish BNN. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The function of the ANN critically relied on zebrafish-like units. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accessibility of the ANN allowed for the discovery of a new temperature responsive cell type in the zebrafish cerebellum. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepKymoTracker outperformed five publicly available automatic analysis tools in almost all respects. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of a seed in DeepKymoTracker allows for better transmission of information about cell position and identity across images. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of a seed in DeepKymoTracker allows for improved tracking by transmitting information about cell position and identity. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepKymoTracker is particularly suited for tracking fast-moving cells in suspension. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepKymoTracker is a pipeline developed for combined tracking and segmentation of cells. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate enhancements to improve tissue-specific prediction performance of DNA accessibility from neural network models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In lung adenocarcinomas, the accessibility perspective uniquely highlights immune pathways that are inversely correlated with a more accessible DNA state.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In lung adenocarcinoma, the accessibility perspective uniquely highlights immune pathways that are inversely correlated with a more open chromatin state.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI-controlled microrobot initially generalizes successfully in 50% of tasks in new environments, improving to over 90% with 30 minutes of further training.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The non-invasive, AI-controlled microrobot offers precise propulsion and efficiently learns from images in data-scarce environments.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The cerebellum can be conceptualized as a reinforcement learning machine.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The implemented cerebellar spiking network successfully learned a state value and solved the mountain car task.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrated the ability to solve the delay eyeblink conditioning task using biologically plausible internal dynamics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach may also be used for other diseases in the future.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the forward-looking claim about applying the approach to other diseases; instead it focuses on methodological contributions and validations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A majority of climbing fiber inputs to Purkinje cells were reduced to only four functional components.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A majority of climbing fiber inputs to Purkinje cells were reduced to only four functional components related to motor initiation, cognitive error-based learning, reward processing, and inhibition of erroneous behaviors.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core finding about dimensionality reduction to four components and associates them with functional roles, consistent with the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These four functional components correspond to accurate timing control of motor initiation related to a Go cue, cognitive error-based learning, reward processing, and inhibition of erroneous behaviors after a No-go cue.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A majority of climbing fiber inputs to Purkinje cells were reduced to only four functional components related to motor initiation, cognitive error-based learning, reward processing, and inhibition of erroneous behaviors.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the mapping of the four components to the same functional roles; content and scope are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spatial distribution of these components coincided well with the boundaries of Aldolase-C/zebrin II expression in Purkinje cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spatial distribution of these components coincided well with boundaries of Aldolase-C/zebrin II expression in Purkinje cells.
alignments.rationale: The spatial correlation with Aldolase-C/zebrin II boundaries is carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synchronization within individual components was bidirectionally regulated according to specific task contexts and learning stages.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Synchronization within individual components was bidirectionally regulated according to specific task contexts and learning stages.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the finding about context- and stage-dependent bidirectional regulation of synchronization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that the cerebellum reduces dimensions by self-organization of components, which may inspire new-generation AI designs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cerebellum reduces dimensions of the learning space by dynamically organizing multiple functional components, which may inspire new-generation AI designs.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines wording ("dynamically organizing" vs "self-organization") but preserves the low-confidence, speculative claim about AI inspiration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model-free deep reinforcement learning method can effectively address various control problems in Probabilistic Boolean Networks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model-free deep reinforcement learning method can effectively address various control problems in probabilistic Boolean networks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q0 restates P0 with effectively identical wording and scope; there is no substantive change in meaning or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method does not rely on the probability transition matrix, making it agnostic to the distribution of probabilities for the next state. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds to the assertion about not relying on the transition matrix or being agnostic to next-state distributions; the claim is not represented in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The time complexity of the deep reinforcement learning approach is linear with respect to the time steps during training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The time complexity of the deep reinforcement learning approach is linear with respect to the time steps or interactions during training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q1 restates the linear time-complexity claim while adding the phrase "or interactions," a small clarification of the metric without changing the original claim's substance or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep reinforcement learning approach demonstrates successful control on large-scale Probabilistic Boolean Networks, including a metastatic melanoma PBN with 200 nodes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep reinforcement learning method successfully controls large-scale probabilistic Boolean networks, including a metastatic melanoma network with 200 nodes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q2 conveys the same result about successful control of a 200-node metastatic melanoma network and large-scale PBNs with essentially identical meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The timing of the dopamine signal relative to reward delivery dictates whether subjective time speeds up or slows down. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The timing of the dopamine signal relative to reward delivery dictates whether subjective time speeds up or slows down. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q0 is effectively identical to P0 in wording and implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Early dopamine signals speed up subjective time and late signals slow it down. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Early dopamine signals speed up subjective time and late signals slow it down. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q1 matches P1 verbatim with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Trial-by-trial changes in the slope of ramping dopamine activity could be predicted from the timing of dopamine activity on the previous trial. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Trial-by-trial changes in the slope of ramping dopamine activity could be predicted from the timing of dopamine activity on the previous trial. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q2 repeats P2 directly and retains the same claim and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This result provides a key piece of evidence supporting a unified computational theory of reinforcement learning and interval timing. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This result provides a key piece of evidence supporting a unified computational theory of reinforcement learning and interval timing. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q3 is the same concluding claim as P3 with no substantive differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with dystonia caused by the mutated TOR1A gene exhibit a risk neutral behaviour compared to controls who are risk averse in the same reinforcement learning task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients with DYT1 dystonia exhibit risk neutral behaviour compared to controls who are risk averse. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: These results suggest that additional factors in patients may lead to divergent effects on D2 receptor dependent cortico-striatal plasticity that are not apparent in rodent models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The relationship between prediction errors and learning rates is nonlinear over the prediction error/learning rates space. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Physiological correlates of learning rates accumulate while learners observe the outcome of their choices and update their beliefs about the environment. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Native mass spectrometry experiments can uncover structural features of ligand interactions, homology models, and point mutations that are undetectable by AlphaFold2 alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
