alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified 636 (12.8%) images that would be difficult for deuteranopes to interpret. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical result is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The frequency of problematic images for deuteranopes has decreased over time. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The frequency of problematic images for deuteranopes has decreased over time. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The temporal trend conclusion is retained with the same framing and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A convolutional neural network classified images with areas under the precision-recall curve of 0.75 and 0.38 for eLife and PubMed Central, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A convolutional neural network classified images from a hold-out test set with an area under the precision-recall curve of 0.75. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a single AUPRC (0.75) on a hold-out set and omits the second (0.38) result, narrowing the claim and tempering the overall reported performance across datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We created a Web application that allows users to upload images, view simulated versions, and obtain predictions regarding color accessibility. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We created a Web application that allows users to upload images, view simulated versions, and obtain predictions regarding color accessibility. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The software contribution is carried over verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pyaging package integrates over 30 aging clocks and plans to expand to more than 100, covering various molecular data types. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: pyaging harmonizes dozens of aging clocks, covering a range of molecular data types such as DNA methylation, transcriptomics, histone mark ChIP-Seq, and ATAC-Seq. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms integration of many aging clocks and specifies covered molecular data types, clarifying and elaborating the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: pyaging utilizes a PyTorch-based backend for GPU acceleration, ensuring rapid inference with large datasets and complex models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention a PyTorch/GPU backend or claims about accelerated inference, so this implementation detail/result is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The package supports multi-species analysis, currently including humans, various mammals, and C. elegans. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: pyaging supports multi-species analysis, extending its utility across various organisms, including humans, various mammals, and Caenorhabditis elegans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the multi-species support claim with equivalent content and slightly higher stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The development of pyaging addresses the lack of robust software tools for integrating and comparing diverse aging models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: pyaging is a comprehensive open-source Python package designed to facilitate aging research. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the contribution as a comprehensive open-source tool to facilitate research, which clarifies and contextualizes the preprint's motivation about addressing tool gaps.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of Gaussian Process State Space Models with numerical approximation of differential equation systems offers a new hybrid model formulation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed hybrid model formulation merges the advantages of Gaussian Process State Space Models and numerical approximation of differential equation systems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same hybrid-model formulation contribution with equivalent emphasis and technical description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed hybrid model demonstrates advantages in modeling fed-batch cultivation for monoclonal antibody production. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The coupling of polynomial regression with Gaussian Process Models demonstrates advantages in fed-batch cultivation for monoclonal antibody production. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication documents advantages in the same application but specifies the particular coupling used (polynomial regression + GP), clarifying the implementation behind the reported advantage.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Handling the dynamics of states in bioprocesses is a major challenge due to continuous time-dependent evolution governed by implicit dynamics. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Handling the dynamics of the states of the system governed by implicit dynamics is a major challenge in modeling complex bioprocesses. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same limitation framing regarding implicit dynamics and state evolution in bioprocess modeling.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Discretization of the process introduces complications such as inability to handle multi-rate data sets and high sensitivity to sampling errors. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate these specific discretization complications, so the preprint's detailed critique of discretization is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning based visual frailty index (vFI) for mice accurately predicts the normalized frailty index score within 0.04 ± 0.002 mean absolute error. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine-learning-based visual frailty index (FI) for mice can accurately predict the normalized FI score within 0.04 ± 0.002 mean absolute error. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the precise accuracy metric and claim for the visual FI unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Features of biological aging are encoded in open-field video data and can be used to construct a vFI that can complement or replace current manual frailty index methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The visual FI provides increased reproducibility and scalability for large-scale mechanistic and interventional studies of aging in mice. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes reproducibility and scalability (complementary strengths) but is more cautious about claims of replacing manual FIs, narrowing and tempering the original stronger suggestion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The vFI provides increased accuracy, reproducibility, and scalability for large scale mechanistic and interventional studies of aging in mice. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The visual FI provides increased reproducibility and scalability for large-scale mechanistic and interventional studies of aging in mice. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains reproducibility and scalability claims but does not assert increased accuracy in the same terms, so the contribution is revised and slightly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Frailty indexes (FIs) are effective predictors of mortality risk in mice. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not reiterate this general statement about FIs predicting mortality risk, so the preprint's standalone claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate a high precision 3D tracking of 10 body parts in several mouse strains with an accuracy of 99.4%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate a high precision 3D tracking of 10 body parts in several mouse strains after focal cerebral ischemia.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the experimental context (after focal cerebral ischemia) making the claim more specific but retains the core result (high precision 3D tracking of 10 body parts at 99.4%).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A comprehensive post-analysis unveils biologically relevant differences in locomotor profiles after a stroke over a time course of three weeks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A comprehensive post-analysis unveils biologically relevant differences in locomotor profiles after a stroke over a time course of 3 weeks.
alignments.rationale: Text and meaning are effectively identical (same period and finding).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning-based motion tracking with comprehensive post-analysis provides accurate and sensitive data to describe the complex recovery of rodents following a stroke.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning-based motion tracking with comprehensive post-analysis provides accurate and sensitive data to describe the complex recovery of rodents following a stroke.
alignments.rationale: Claim is repeated verbatim and retains the same conclusion and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental set-up and analysis can also benefit a range of other neurological injuries that affect locomotion.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental set-up and analysis can also benefit a range of other neurological injuries that affect locomotion.
alignments.rationale: Publication carries the same contribution statement with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new algorithm based on deep learning for ECoG-FM achieves an accuracy comparable to ESM in identifying eloquent language cortex.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new algorithm based on deep learning for ECoG-FM achieves an accuracy comparable to ESM in identifying eloquent language cortex.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint result with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm resulted in a 23% improvement over conventional ECoG-FM analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm obtained an accuracy of 83.05% in identifying language regions, representing a 23% improvement over conventional ECoG-FM analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the 23% improvement but integrates it with the explicit 83.05% accuracy figure, clarifying the numerical context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The state-of-the-art accuracy of 83.05% in identifying language regions has never been achieved before.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm obtained an accuracy of 83.05% in identifying language regions, representing a 23% improvement over conventional ECoG-FM analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the accuracy value but omits the novelty assertion ('never been achieved before'), reducing the novelty claim and tempering confidence about precedence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning powered ECoG-FM can serve as a stand-alone modality and avoid likely hazards of ESM in epilepsy surgery.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning powered ECoG-FM can serve as a stand-alone modality and avoid likely hazards of ESM in epilepsy surgery.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about serving as a stand-alone modality is carried unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of the new algorithm reduces the potential for developing post-surgical morbidity in language function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of the new algorithm reduces the potential for developing post-surgical morbidity in language function.
alignments.rationale: Publication preserves this clinical implication with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: StrucTFactor is the first method to utilize 3D secondary structural information of proteins for transcription factor prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: StrucTFactor is the first method to utilize 3D secondary structural information of proteins for transcription factor prediction.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: StrucTFactor significantly outperforms existing transcription factor prediction methods with a p-value of less than 0.001.
alignments.pub_claim_text: StrucTFactor significantly outperforms existing transcription factor prediction methods, with a P-value of less than 0.001.
alignments.rationale: Statistical performance claim is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: StrucTFactor improves performance over its closest competitor by up to 17% based on Matthews correlation coefficient.
alignments.pub_claim_text: StrucTFactor improves performance over its closest competitor by up to 17% based on Matthews correlation coefficient.
alignments.rationale: Performance improvement vs competitor is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Plasmodium parasites have developed resistance against many front-line drugs, threatening global malaria eradication efforts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Plasmodium parasites have developed resistance against many front-line drugs, threatening global malaria eradication efforts.
alignments.rationale: High-level conclusion about drug resistance and its threat is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis of the protein-protein interaction (PPI) network revealed key proteins vital for the survival of Plasmodium falciparum.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis of the protein-protein interaction (PPI) network revealed key proteins vital for the survival of Plasmodium falciparum.
alignments.rationale: The result identifying key proteins via PPI analysis is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A set of potential drug molecules was computationally predicted based on the identification of strong binding sites on key proteins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A set of potential drug molecules was computationally predicted using a deep learning-based technique based on the identification of strong binding sites on key proteins.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds that a deep learning–based technique was used to make the predictions, specifying the computational approach but not changing the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides a general computational pipeline to identify crucial proteins using single-cell transcriptomic data sets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides a general computational pipeline to identify crucial proteins using single-cell RNA sequencing data sets.
alignments.rationale: 'Single-cell transcriptomic' is made explicit as single-cell RNA-seq in the publication, a clarification of the data type rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inference of the D–GEX method can be improved using a logistic sigmoid activation function instead of the hyperbolic tangent.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim comparing logistic sigmoid vs hyperbolic tangent in D–GEX does not appear in the publication; the publication focuses on the new transformative adaptive function instead.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel transformative adaptive activation function has been proposed that further improves gene expression inference.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed transformative adaptive activation function improves gene expression inference significantly.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the proposed transformative adaptive activation function claim and emphasizes significant improvement; confidence is slightly amplified by stronger wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The improved neural network achieves an average mean absolute error of 0.1340, which is a significant improvement over the original D–GEX's average mean absolute error of 0.1637.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The improved neural network achieves an average mean absolute error of 0.1340, which is a significant improvement over the original D-GEX's average mean absolute error of 0.1637.
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance comparison is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed transformative adaptive function enables a significantly more accurate reconstruction of full gene expression profiles with only a small increase in model complexity.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a new claim about the small increase in model complexity enabling more accurate reconstruction, which was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differential expression analysis revealed a distinct colonic transcriptome for patients who developed strictures, with downregulation of pathways related to inflammation and extra-cellular matrix production.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Differential expression analysis revealed downregulation of pathways related to inflammation and extra-cellular matrix production in patients with strictures.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same differential-expression finding focused specifically on the downregulated pathways; the broader phrase 'distinct colonic transcriptome' is de-emphasized, so the claim is clarified and slightly narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning-based models were able to incorporate colonic gene expression and clinical characteristics to predict outcomes with high accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning-based models can predict outcomes in pediatric Crohn's disease with high accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion about ML models predicting outcomes with high accuracy, matching the preprint claim closely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models showed an AUROC of 0.84 for strictures, 0.83 for remission, and 0.75 for surgery.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.84 for strictures, 0.83 for remission, and 0.75 for surgery.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same performance metrics verbatim, so this result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Certain genes with potential prognostic importance for strictures (REG1A, MMP3, and DUOX2) were not identified in single gene differential analysis but were found to have strong contributions to predictive models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Genes with potential prognostic importance for strictures were not identified in single gene differential analysis but were found to contribute strongly to predictive models.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same finding about genes missed by single-gene DE but captured by models; content and emphasis are equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings in FFPE tissue support the importance of colonic gene expression and the potential for machine learning-based models in predicting outcomes for pediatric CD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning-based models can predict outcomes in pediatric Crohn's disease with high accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's broader statement linking FFPE findings to model utility is distilled in the publication to the direct claim that ML models can predict outcomes; the core conclusion is preserved but made more explicit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning algorithm achieved a sensitivity of 87% and a specificity of 80% for the prediction of above- vs. below-median survival on the unseen data of the external validation cohort.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning algorithm achieved 87% sensitivity and 80% specificity for predicting above- versus below-median overall survival in the independent validation cohort.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same sensitivity/specificity result for the independent validation cohort verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Heterogeneity-related features were highly ranked by the model.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not report the specific point about heterogeneity-related features being highly ranked; that detail appears omitted in the final claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Of the 21 patients for whom the histopathological subtype was determined, 8/9 patients predicted by the model to experience below-median overall survival exhibited the quasi-mesenchymal subtype.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A significant association was found between histopathological subtype and overall survival prediction, with 8 out of 9 patients predicted to experience below-median overall survival exhibiting the quasi-mesenchymal subtype.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same association and the identical 8/9 observation, so the claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of machine-learning to the radiomic analysis of DW-MRI-derived ADC maps allowed the prediction of overall survival with high diagnostic accuracy in a prospectively collected cohort.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ROC-AUC for the prediction of above- versus below-median overall survival was 90% in the independent validation cohort.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's general conclusion about high diagnostic accuracy is supported in the publication by an explicit ROC-AUC (90%); the publication provides a specific performance metric that clarifies the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The high overlap of clinically relevant histopathological subtypes with model predictions underlines the potential of quantitative imaging workflows in pre-operative subtyping and risk assessment in PDAC.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The high overlap of clinically relevant histopathological subtypes with model predictions indicates the potential of quantitative imaging in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma pre-operative subtyping and prognosis.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same conclusion about the potential of quantitative imaging workflows given the overlap with histopathological subtypes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine learning approach using cfDNA achieved high sensitivity and specificity in a large, predominantly early-stage, colorectal cancer cohort.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning approach using cfDNA achieved high sensitivity and specificity in a large, predominantly early-stage, colorectal cancer cohort.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level result about cfDNA ML performance in an early-stage CRC cohort.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In a colorectal cancer cohort heavily weighted towards early-stage cancer, we achieved a mean AUC of 0.92 with a mean sensitivity of 85% at 85% specificity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In a colorectal cancer cohort heavily weighted towards early-stage cancer, we achieved a mean AUC of 0.92 with a mean sensitivity of 85% at 85% specificity.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the numerical performance metrics exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sensitivity generally increased with tumor stage and increasing tumor fraction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sensitivity generally increased with tumor stage and increasing tumor fraction.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion relating sensitivity to stage and tumor fraction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The possibility of systematic technical and institution-specific biases warrants similar confounder analyses in other studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The possibility of systematic technical and institution-specific biases warrants similar confounder analyses in other studies.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the caution about technical and institutional confounders requiring similar analyses.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prospective validation of this machine learning method and evaluation of a multi-analyte approach are underway.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Prospective validation of this machine learning method and evaluation of a multi-analyte approach are underway.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the prospective validation statement but lists it with higher asserted certainty (medium vs low in the preprint), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed supervised multi-modal deep learning model predicts tissue/cell type-specific genome-wide promoter-enhancer and promoter-promoter interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed supervised multi-modal deep learning model outperforms state-of-the-art deep learning methods in predicting promoter-centered chromatin interactions.
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey that the developed model predicts promoter-centered interactions; the publication emphasizes benchmarking but the core predictive capability is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach outperforms state-of-the-art deep learning methods and is robust to the inclusion of anchor distance as a feature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed supervised multi-modal deep learning model outperforms state-of-the-art deep learning methods in predicting promoter-centered chromatin interactions.
alignments.rationale: The outperformance portion is retained in the publication, but the preprint's specific claim about robustness to anchor-distance inclusion is not reflected in the listed publication claims, so the claim is revised (partial loss of detail).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach achieves comparable prediction performance using biologically relevant tissues/cell types compared to using all tissues/cell types, particularly for predicting promoter-enhancer interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach achieves comparable prediction performance using predefined biologically relevant tissues/cell types compared to using all tissues/cell types in the pretraining.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about comparable performance when using biologically relevant tissue/cell-type subsets in pretraining.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models have shown performance improvements over alternatives for sequence-based promoter recognition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reliability of deep learning ab initio promoter recognition models on eukaryotic genomic sequences is still not at a sufficient level, as overall performance is still low.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized relative performance gains; the publication reframes the picture to stress low overall reliability, tempering the original positive framing and narrowing the claim to absolute performance limitations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lack of a consensus dataset and procedure for benchmarking has made it difficult to assess the true performance of promoter recognition models.
alignments.rationale: The publication claims do not explicitly restate the lack-of-consensus-dataset point; that specific benchmarking-availability critique appears omitted from the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Supervised Promoter Recognition Framework (SUPR REF) streamlines the process of training, validating, testing, and comparing promoter recognition models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Supervised Promoter Recognition Framework ('SUPR REF') streamlines the process of training, validating, testing, and comparing promoter recognition models.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same contribution statement about SUPR REF.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reliability of deep learning ab initio promoter recognition models on eukaryotic genomic sequences is still not at a sufficient level, as precision is severely lacking.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reliability of deep learning ab initio promoter recognition models on eukaryotic genomic sequences is still not at a sufficient level, as overall performance is still low.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion about insufficient reliability but states it in slightly broader terms ('overall performance is still low') rather than focusing only on precision, so the claim is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cross-validation results from small datasets need to be interpreted with caution due to the observational nature of the data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cross-validation results from small promoter datasets need to be interpreted with caution due to the observational nature of the data.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the caution about interpreting cross-validation results from small promoter datasets, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Absolut! software suite enables the generation of synthetic lattice-based 3D-antibody-antigen binding structures with ground-truth access to conformational paratope, epitope, and affinity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Absolut! software suite enables parameter-based unconstrained generation of synthetic lattice-based three-dimensional antibody–antigen-binding structures.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the generation capability but omits the explicit claim of providing ground-truth access to paratope/epitope/affinity, reducing the scope and tempering the confidence in that specific ground-truth claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Common immunological antibody specificity prediction problems have been formalized as machine learning tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Common immunological antibody-specificity prediction problems have been formalized as machine learning tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this formalization with essentially identical wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accuracy-based rankings of machine learning methods trained on experimental data hold for methods trained on Absolut!-generated data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Accuracy-based rankings of machine learning methods trained on experimental data hold for methods trained on Absolut!-generated data.
alignments.rationale: The result is carried over verbatim from preprint to publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Absolut! framework enables real-world relevant development and benchmarking of machine learning strategies for biotherapeutics design.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Absolut! framework has the potential to enable real-world relevant development and benchmarking of machine learning strategies for biotherapeutics design.
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the claim by adding "has the potential to," which clarifies and makes the conclusion more tentative.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Relative machine learning performance learnt on Absolut! datasets transfers to experimental datasets.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about transfer of relative ML performance to experimental datasets is not present in the publication and appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An analogous mechanism to random dropout in artificial neural networks may exist in biological neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Serotonergic fibers can potentially implement a dropout-like mechanism in brain tissue, supporting neuroplasticity.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the general preprint idea more specific by identifying serotonergic fibers and linking the mechanism to neuroplasticity; the claim is stated more confidently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The brain serotonergic fibers meet some expected criteria for a dropout mechanism due to their ubiquitous presence, stochastic structure, and ability to grow throughout an individual's lifespan.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The brain serotonergic fibers meet some of the expected criteria for a dropout mechanism due to their ubiquitous presence, stochastic structure, and ability to grow throughout the individual's lifespan.
alignments.rationale: The publication echoes this result but downgrades certainty slightly ("some of the expected criteria" vs the stronger tone in the preprint).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trajectories of serotonergic fibers can be modeled as paths of anomalous diffusion processes.
alignments.rationale: The specific modeling result about anomalous diffusion of fiber trajectories is not explicitly asserted in the publication and appears to have been omitted or de-emphasized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research contributes to biologically-inspired regularization in artificial neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mathematical theories of the structure and dynamics of serotonergic fibers can contribute to the design of dropout algorithms in ANNs.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution more concretely as mathematical theories informing dropout algorithm design, using slightly more cautious language and lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: STRING2GO successfully adopts deep maxout neural networks to learn functional representations that encode both protein-protein interactions and functional predictive information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: STRING2GO successfully adopts deep maxout neural networks to learn functional representations that encode both protein-protein interactions and functional predictive information.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this methodological contribution verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: STRING2GO outperforms other network embedding-based prediction methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: STRING2GO outperforms other protein-protein interaction network-based prediction methods.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is maintained with equivalent scope and confidence in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: STRING2GO outperforms one benchmark method adopted in a recent large scale protein function prediction competition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: STRING2GO outperforms one benchmark method adopted in a recent large scale protein function prediction competition.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries over the benchmark comparison claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: StomataCounter is an automated stomata counting system that uses a deep convolutional neural network to identify stomata in various microscopic images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: StomataCounter is an automated stomata counting system that uses a deep convolutional neural network.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core contribution; the preprint's additional detail about various microscopic images is implicit and not contradicted, so the claim is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network achieves 98.1% identification accuracy on Ginkgo SEM micrographs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network achieves 98.1% identification accuracy on Ginkgo scanning electron microscopy micrographs.
alignments.rationale: The reported accuracy on Ginkgo SEM images is carried over directly with matching wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system demonstrates 94.2% transfer accuracy when tested on untrained species.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system demonstrates 94.2% transfer accuracy when tested on untrained species.
alignments.rationale: The transfer accuracy result is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method is made publicly available to facilitate adoption.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the explicit claim about public availability as presented in the preprint; that specific claim appears to be omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: StomataCounter addresses the lack of user-friendly, fully automated methods for identifying and counting stomata.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit positioning statement about addressing a gap in user-friendly automated methods that was not stated as a claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our pipeline of Deep Learning Models accurately identifies dividing cells in time-lapse movies of epithelial tissues in vivo.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed pipeline of deep learning models accurately identifies dividing cells in time-lapse movies of epithelial tissues in vivo.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the pipeline's accuracy in identifying dividing cells with essentially identical wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The division axis is biased according to lines of tissue tension.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The division axis is biased according to lines of tissue tension.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this specific result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Wounding triggers a synchronised (but not oriented) burst of cell divisions back from the leading edge.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Wounding triggers a synchronised (but not oriented) burst of cell divisions back from the leading edge.
alignments.rationale: The wounding-induced synchronized burst result is carried over verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spatio-temporal cell division analyses following wounding reveal spatial synchronicity that scales with wound size.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim that the spatial synchronicity scales with wound size does not appear in the publication and thus seems to have been omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The pipeline determines the axis of division orientation and shape changes of cells before and after division.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit capability of the pipeline to determine division axis and pre/post-division shape changes that was not claimed in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DMISO showed high precision (95%) and recall (90%) based on 10-fold cross-validation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication retains the integration claim but provides details on delivery formats; it clarifies how integration is achieved (formats/services).

alignments.pub_claim_text: CAPICE is a new machine-learning-based method for prioritizing pathogenic variants, including SNVs and short InDels.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a descriptive claim about the variant types covered (SNVs and short InDels) that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PDIVAS identifies around 27 pathogenic candidates per individual with 95% sensitivity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: When PDIVAS was applied to genome sequencing analysis on a threshold with 95% sensitivity for reported pathogenic SAVs, an average of 27 pathogenic candidates were extracted per individual.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same numeric result and sensitivity threshold, preserving the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PDIVAS prioritizes causative variants more efficiently than previous predictors in simulated patient genome sequences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PDIVAS showed an average precision of 0.92 and a maximum MCC of 0.88 in classifying these variants, which were the best of the previous predictors.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides explicit performance metrics demonstrating PDIVAS outperformed prior predictors, which clarifies the preprint's efficiency claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep-intronic variants are often overlooked in whole-genome sequencing analyses due to their difficulty in segregation from benign variants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating PDIVAS into variant interpretation pipelines will enable efficient detection of disease-causing deep-intronic SAVs and contribute to improving the diagnostic yield.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on the tool's utility in detecting deep-intronic SAVs and improving diagnostic yield rather than explicitly stating they are often overlooked; this shifts the emphasis from problem statement to solution.

alignments.pub_claim_text: PDIVAS was trained on an ensemble machine-learning algorithm to classify pathogenic and benign variants in a curated dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces specific methodological/training details about PDIVAS that were not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified 20 common genetic loci associated with left atrial traits in a genome-wide association study.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identify 20 common genetic loci associated with left atrial structure and function.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result (20 loci) with equivalent scope (structure/function) and no meaningful change in certainty or interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A Mendelian randomization analysis confirmed that atrial fibrillation causally affects left atrial volume.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We find evidence supporting a causal role for left atrial enlargement and dysfunction on atrial fibrillation risk.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a causal relationship but in the opposite direction (left atrial enlargement causing AF rather than AF causing left atrial volume changes), representing a substantive revision of the original directional claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A polygenic prediction of left atrial minimum volume was significantly associated with the risk for atrial fibrillation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Polygenic contributions to increased left atrial volume are associated with atrial fibrillation and its downstream consequences, including stroke.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the polygenic association with AF and expands the scope by explicitly linking increased LA volume to downstream consequences (e.g., stroke), clarifying and broadening the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We found an inverse relation between genetic variants associated with atrial fibrillation risk and left atrial emptying fraction.
alignments.rationale: The specific finding about an inverse relationship with left atrial emptying fraction is not reflected in the publication claims and appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI system predicted MCI/mild AD in the full sample with an AUC of 0.85.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI system predicted MCI/mild AD with an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.85.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same AUC result for MCI/mild AD prediction with equivalent certainty and framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI system demonstrated the ability to differentiate between amyloid beta positive and negative subjects as well as subjects with mild cognitive impairment or mild AD from cognitively healthy subjects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI system predicted Aβ positivity in clinical subsamples, with AUCs of 0.78 for MCI/mild AD and 0.74 for cognitively unimpaired participants.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies that Aβ prediction was successful only in clinical subsamples (not the full sample) and provides AUCs; this narrows and tempers the original, broader preprint claim about overall differentiation ability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study supports the real-world applicability of speech-based assessment to detect early stage Alzheimer’s disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Speech-based testing offers simple and accessible screening for early-stage Alzheimer's disease.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion about applicability for early-stage screening, phrased to emphasize simplicity and accessibility, without materially changing the claim's scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research is the first to demonstrate the use case and promise of speech-based AI systems for clinical practice in detecting early stage Alzheimer’s disease.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not assert novelty or a 'first demonstration' claim present in the preprint; that contribution claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sensitive speech-based biomarkers may help to fulfill the unmet need for scalable and accessible testing for early cognitive impairment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Speech-based testing offers simple and accessible screening for early-stage Alzheimer's disease.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same idea about scalable, accessible screening via speech-based testing, framed more concretely as offering simple screening for early-stage disease.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The view classifier demonstrated an AUC of 0.998 in the held-out CSMC test set for identifying MR color Doppler videos.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The view classifier demonstrated an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.998 in the Cedars-Sinai Medical Center test set for identifying echocardiography videos with color Doppler information across the mitral valve.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high AUC result for identifying color Doppler mitral-view videos with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the external test cohort from SHC, the view classifier correctly identified 1,051 of 1,055 MR color Doppler videos with a sensitivity of 0.996.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the external test cohort from Stanford Healthcare, the view classifier correctly identified 1051 of 1055 manually curated videos with color Doppler information across the mitral valve.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the external validation result with the same counts and high sensitivity, matching the preprint's claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model detected moderate-or-severe MR with an AUC of 0.916 in the CSMC test cohort.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated pipeline detected moderate or greater mitral regurgitation (MR) severity with an AUC of 0.916 in the Cedars-Sinai Medical Center test cohort.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same AUC result for detecting moderate-or-greater MR in the same test cohort.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An automated pipeline for identifying clinically significant MR from transthoracic echocardiography studies was developed and validated.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study presents a novel automated pipeline for identifying clinically significant MR from full transthoracic echocardiography studies.
alignments.rationale: Both describe the development and validation of the same automated pipeline; the publication frames it as novel but retains the same substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach has potential for automated screening of MR and precision evaluation for surveillance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated pipeline has the potential for automated screening and surveillance of mitral regurgitation.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same potential for screening and surveillance of MR, matching the preprint's forward-looking conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning models trained on digitized chest radiographs performed well in identifying abnormalities suggestive of tuberculosis, achieving an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.97 on the internal test dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning models trained on digitized chest radiographs performed well in identifying abnormalities suggestive of tuberculosis, achieving an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.97 on the internal test dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact internal AUC result and framing, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models demonstrated a −2% absolute percentage error in estimating sample-level counts of abnormal radiographs on the internal test dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models demonstrated strong performance in estimating sample-level counts of abnormal radiographs with an absolute percentage error of -2% on the internal test dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same sample-level estimation performance (−2% APE) with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On external test datasets, the models maintained strong performance in identifying both generic abnormalities and those suggestive of tuberculosis, with AUCs ranging from 0.89 to 0.99.
alignments.pub_claim_text: On external test datasets, the models maintained high performance in identifying generic abnormalities and those suggestive of tuberculosis, with AUCs ranging from 0.89 to 0.99.
alignments.rationale: External AUC range and performance characterization are repeated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the models was consistent across various metrics, including sensitivity, specificity, and F1 score.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The consistent performance of the models across various metrics indicates their potential as reliable tools for supporting chest radiography quality control activities at the CDC.
alignments.rationale: The publication links the consistency across metrics to potential practical utility (CDC quality control), clarifying the implication of the metric-level consistency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The strong performance of the models relative to high-quality radiological reference standards suggests they may serve as reliable tools for supporting chest radiography quality control activities at the CDC.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The consistent performance of the models across various metrics indicates their potential as reliable tools for supporting chest radiography quality control activities at the CDC.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same applied claim about serving as reliable tools for CDC quality control; wording shifts slightly but the claim is retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm developed demonstrates a Dice similarity coefficient (DSC) of 0.527 in external testing for segmenting leukoaraiosis (LA) on CT.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm developed for leukoaraiosis segmentation on CT achieved a Dice similarity coefficient of 0.527 in the external test set.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same DSC result for external testing with identical substance and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predicted LA volumes from the deep learning algorithm are significantly correlated with Fazekas grade in both external testing and US datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predicted leukoaraiosis volumes showed high correlations with registered leukoaraiosis (r = 0.953) and white matter hyperintensity (r = 0.951).
alignments.rationale: The publication provides specific correlation coefficients and references to registered measures, clarifying and quantifying the preprint's statement about high correlations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm shows consistent performance across different CT vendors with correlation coefficients ranging from 0.875 to 0.950.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report vendor-specific performance ranges; this preprint detail appears omitted in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In an independent clinical dataset, the predicted LA volumes correlate with age and clinical outcomes after ischemic stroke.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In an independent clinical cohort, leukoaraiosis volume was independently associated with age, vascular risk factors, and 3-month functional outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the association with age and clinical outcomes and further specifies additional associated factors (vascular risk factors), clarifying and slightly expanding the reported associations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm provides a reproducible method for LA segmentation on CT, improving the assessment of ischemic stroke patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm provides a reproducible method for leukoaraiosis segmentation on CT, enhancing the assessment of ischemic stroke patients.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the conclusion that the algorithm is a reproducible segmentation method that enhances assessment of ischemic stroke patients, matching the preprint's claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best model that autoML generated using all 47 variables was the stacked ensemble model of all models with an AUCPR of 0.836. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best model generated by using autoML and 48 variables was the stacked ensemble model with an AUPRC of 0.807. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both describe the same best model (stacked ensemble) but report different variable counts and lower AUPRC in the publication, indicating a numeric revision and reduced performance confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most influential variables identified in predicting patient mortality from COVID-19 were systolic and diastolic blood pressure, age, respiratory rate, pulse oximetry, blood urea nitrogen, lactate dehydrogenase, d-dimer, troponin, and glucose. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 10 most influential variables for generating high-performing models included systolic and diastolic blood pressure, age, and several laboratory values. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same core influential variables but with less itemized detail ('several laboratory values'), which clarifies and narrows the specificity of the original list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ML model developed can be used as a decision supporting tool for medical practitioners to efficiently triage COVID-19 infected patients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoML is an efficient, effective, and informative method for generating machine learning-based clinical decision support tools. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert usefulness for clinical decision support; the publication frames it as a characterization of autoML (efficient/effective/informative), clarifying the original conclusion about clinical application.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Retraining the autoML with the 10 most important variables did not significantly affect the performance, with an AUCPR of 0.828. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the retraining-with-10-variables performance or the 0.828 AUCPR, so this specific retraining result appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study represents the largest COVID-19 patient cohort to train ML models and the first to utilize autoML. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not assert the 'largest cohort' or 'first to utilize autoML' claims, so this contribution statement is not present in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified clinically relevant predictors of gait speed reserve (GSR), usual gait speed (UGS), and maximum gait speed (MGS) using a feature selection pipeline. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Features common to all models predicting UGS, maximum gait speed (MGS), and gait speed reserve (GSR) include age, grip strength, chair stands time, mean motor reaction time, and height. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements report identification of relevant predictors for the three gait outcomes; the publication restates the predictors identified by the study.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MGS and UGS models were found to be more explainable than the GSR model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not explicitly compare model explainability between MGS/UGS and GSR, so this comparative explainability claim does not appear in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Common features across all three models include age, grip strength, chair stands time, mean motor reaction time in the choice reaction time test, and height. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Features common to all models predicting UGS, maximum gait speed (MGS), and gait speed reserve (GSR) include age, grip strength, chair stands time, mean motor reaction time, and height. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same list of common features across the three models, essentially unchanged in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings support a network physiology approach to understanding predictors of performance-based tasks in older adults. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings may help provide new insights into the multisystem predictors of gait speed and gait speed reserve in older adults. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the conclusion more cautiously ('may help provide new insights') than the preprint's stronger 'support' phrasing, clarifying and slightly tempering the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of an explainable machine learning technique may provide clinicians with new insights into the multisystem predictors of gait speed and gait speed reserve. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings may help provide new insights into the multisystem predictors of gait speed and gait speed reserve in older adults. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both claims state that the study's methods/findings can provide new insights into multisystem predictors; the publication preserves this contribution-oriented statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Children with dyslexia show reduced evidence accumulation compared to typically developing children in visual motion processing tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Children with dyslexia exhibit reduced evidence accumulation compared to typically developing children in visual motion processing tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same empirical finding with essentially identical meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A response-locked EEG component was identified that correlates with reduced drift-rate in children with dyslexia. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A response-locked EEG component indicates a neural correlate of reduced drift rate in dyslexia during the motion coherence task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result linking a response-locked EEG component to reduced drift rate in dyslexia.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Children with dyslexia are slower to extract sensory evidence from global motion displays, regardless of the need to segregate signal-from-noise. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Children with dyslexia are slower to extract sensory evidence from global motion displays, regardless of noise exclusion requirements. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about slower sensory evidence extraction is preserved verbatim in meaning between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed SurvMaximin algorithm achieves comparable or higher accuracy compared to estimators using only the information of the target site and other existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed SurvMaximin algorithm achieves comparable or higher accuracy compared to estimators using only the information of the target site and other existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim about SurvMaximin without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SurvMaximin estimator is robust to variations in sample sizes and estimated feature coefficients between centers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SurvMaximin estimator is robust to variations in sample sizes and estimated feature coefficients between centers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The robustness claim is maintained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SurvMaximin provides robust Cox feature coefficient estimates without outcome information in the target population and is privacy-preserving. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SurvMaximin provides robust Cox feature coefficient estimates without outcome information in the target population and is privacy-preserving. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim about providing robust feature estimates without target outcomes and being privacy-preserving.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SurvMaximin method is well suited for both federated and transfer learning in the high-dimensional survival analysis setting. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SurvMaximin method is well suited for both federated and transfer learning in high-dimensional survival analysis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts the same suitability claim but with higher stated certainty, representing an amplification of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CatBoost model attained an AUROC of 0.862, which is an increase from an initial AUROC of 0.821, the best reported in the literature. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CatBoost model attained an AUROC of 0.862, which is an increase from an initial AUROC of 0.821, the best reported in the literature. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same AUROC improvement claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CatBoost model demonstrated an accuracy of 0.789 and an F1-score of 0.747, outperforming other models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CatBoost model demonstrated an accuracy of 0.789 and an F1-score of 0.747, outperforming other models evaluated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance metrics and comparative claim appear unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The preprocessing methodology significantly reduced the number of relevant features and identified critical features previously overlooked. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The preprocessing methodology significantly reduced the number of relevant features and identified critical features previously overlooked. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the preprocessing claim verbatim in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating the identified features and tuning the parameters allowed our model to demonstrate strong generalization to unseen data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating the identified features and tuning the parameters allowed the model to demonstrate strong generalization to unseen data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The generalization claim is preserved between versions with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights the potential of machine learning as a crucial tool in ICUs for enhancing resource allocation and providing personalized interventions for MV patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the potential of machine learning as a crucial tool in ICUs for enhancing resource allocation and providing personalized interventions for MV patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this broader clinical implication claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Random Forest classifier provides a classification accuracy of 99.7%, precision of 99.8%, sensitivity of 98.8%, and F-score of 99.29% for predicting the Infection Susceptibility Probability (ISP) score of COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The random forest classifier provides a classification accuracy of 99.7%, precision of 99.8%, sensitivity of 98.8%, and F-score of 99.29% for the given medical dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance metrics and claim are essentially identical between preprint and publication, with only minor wording differences (ISP score vs given medical dataset).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed machine learning approach enables individuals to predict their susceptibility to COVID-19 infection and take precautionary measures. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed machine learning approach can help individuals take additional precautions for protecting themselves from COVID-19 infection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the individual-level benefit in softer language and with lower certainty, clarifying and tempering the original high-certainty claim about enabling prediction and action.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clinicians and government officials can focus on highly susceptible individuals to limit the spread of the COVID-19 pandemic based on the results of the proposed method. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clinicians and government officials can focus on the highly susceptible people for limiting the pandemic spread based on the results of the proposed method. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same policy-oriented conclusion with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Random Forest classifier demonstrates improved classification performance compared to Support Vector Regression for the given health care data set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The random forest classifier demonstrates improved classification performance compared to support vector regression for the given healthcare dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result comparing random forest to SVR is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gut dysbiosis is consistently associated with mortality in solid organ transplant recipients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gut dysbiosis is consistently associated with mortality in solid organ transplant recipients (SOTR). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion that gut dysbiosis is associated with mortality in SOTR is maintained verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified two overall microbial community variation patterns that are associated with all-cause mortality independent of the organ transplant. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gut microbiome distance between each transplantation recipient to the average of the general population was associated with all-cause mortality and death from infection, malignancy, and cardiovascular disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a specific measurement (distance to population average) rather than the preprint's description of 'two overall microbial community variation patterns', indicating a revision in how the pattern is characterized though the association result remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multiple indicators of gut dysbiosis predict all-cause mortality and death by cardiovascular diseases, malignancy, and infection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gut microbiome distance between each transplantation recipient to the average of the general population was associated with all-cause mortality and death from infection, malignancy, and cardiovascular disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames multiple dysbiosis indicators in terms of microbiome distance to a population average, clarifying the specific indicator used to predict these causes of death.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified 23 species that were associated with all-cause mortality using multivariable Cox regression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A multivariable Cox regression identified 23 bacterial species that were associated with all-cause mortality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about 23 species identified by multivariable Cox regression is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A log-ratio of 19 bacterial species was identified as associated with all-cause mortality using a machine learning algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning algorithm identified a balance consisting of 19 out of the 23 species that were associated with all-cause mortality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies that the 19-species signal is a balance derived from the 23 species set identified earlier, keeping the main result but making its relationship to the 23 species explicit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPT-4 correctly diagnosed the condition in 28% of the images and included the correct diagnosis in its list of top differentials for 48% of the images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific overall accuracy/top-differential percentages reported in the preprint are not present in the publication and appear to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPT-4 exhibited better performance in providing the correct diagnosis for lighter skin tones (44%) compared to darker skin tones (12%), which was statistically significant (p-value < 0.05). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GPT-4 exhibited better performance in providing the correct diagnosis for lighter skin tones compared with darker skin tones. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core result about superior performance for lighter skin tones; the significance detail is also reported elsewhere in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With each unit increase in the Fitzpatrick scale, GPT-4's performance decreased by 11.4% in accurately providing a differential diagnosis and by 7.1% in accurately providing the correct diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: With each unit increase in the Fitzpatrick scale, GPT-4’s performance decreased by 11.4% in accurately providing a differential diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the 11.4% per-unit decrease for differential diagnosis; the preprint combined two related per-unit statistics, which the publication split across claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPT-4 exhibited significantly lower overall accuracy compared to previous studies reporting accuracies as high as 90%. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The comparative statement about prior studies' overall accuracy is not present in the publication and appears to have been dropped.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study is limited by its relatively small sample size, which could impact the generalizability of the findings. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The specific limitation about small sample size mentioned in the preprint does not appear explicitly in the publication's listed claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Large language models (LLMs) with intentional prompting can match human screening performance in systematic reviews. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Optimized prompts using GPT4-0125-preview achieved a weighted sensitivity of 97.7% and specificity of 85.2% in abstract screening. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides concrete performance metrics in abstract screening, narrowing and clarifying the preprint's broader claim about matching human screening performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Framework Chain-of-Thought prompting approach achieved a mean accuracy of 93.6% and sensitivity of 97.5% in full-text screening. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Optimized prompts using GPT4-0125-preview achieved a weighted sensitivity of 97.7% and specificity of 85.2% in abstract screening. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports different metrics and focuses on abstract (not full-text) screening with a different prompt/setting, indicating a revision in the reported performance and context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our full-text prompt demonstrated significantly higher sensitivity in four reviews and significantly higher accuracy in one review compared to experienced reviewers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A generic prompt for abstract and full-text screening achieving high sensitivity and specificity that can be adapted to other SRs and LLMs was developed. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's comparative full-text findings into a more general claim about a transferable prompt that attains high sensitivity/specificity, clarifying and generalizing the original comparative result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Traditional human screening for a systematic review of 7000 articles required 530 hours and $10,000 USD, while our approach completed screening in one day for $430 USD. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The LLM-based approach completed screening in under 1 day for $157.02 USD, compared to over 83 hours and $1666.67 USD for single human abstract screening. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication updates the time and cost figures (showing even greater efficiency and lower cost) and frames them for abstract screening specifically, which revises and amplifies the efficiency claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Zero-shot prompts had poor sensitivity of 49.0% in abstract screening and 49.1% in full-text screening. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The zero-shot performance result appears in the publication but was not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Further prompt optimizations may exist, indicating limitations in the current study. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication's explicit statement about possible further prompt optimizations (a limitation) is not present in the preprint claims and is therefore an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating large language models (LLMs) into healthcare settings can improve workflow efficiency and patient care by automating tasks such as summarising consultations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
