alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the demonstration as a potential facilitator of precision diagnosis contingent on further validation, which is a more cautious/qualified presentation of the preprint's conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further replication of this machine learning tool in a real-world context or a clinical trial will facilitate its implementation in the clinic. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning tools could facilitate the implementation of precision diagnosis in the clinic with further validation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same recommendation that further validation is needed to facilitate clinical implementation, matching the preprint's suggestion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Four computable phenotypes (PedSep-A, B, C, and D) were derived from 25 bedside clinical variables in 404 children with severe sepsis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four computable phenotypes (PedSep-A, B, C, and D) are derived from the PHENOMS dataset.
alignments.rationale: Both claims report the same core result (four phenotypes derived); the publication names the dataset (PHENOMS) and omits the specific variable/count details, which is a clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PedSep-D has the highest likelihood of Thrombocytopenia Associated Multiple Organ Failure and Macrophage Activation Syndrome.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PedSep-D patients have the highest likelihood of developing thrombocytopenia-associated multiple organ failure and macrophage activation syndrome.
alignments.rationale: Textual differences are minor (wording/capitalization); the scientific content and confidence remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Membership in PedSep-D appears optimal for enrollment in early anti-inflammatory trials targeting Thrombocytopenia Associated Multiple Organ Failure and Macrophage Activation Syndrome.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PedSep-D is optimal for enrollment in early personalized anti-inflammatory trials targeting thrombocytopenia-associated multiple organ failure and macrophage activation syndrome in pediatric sepsis.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same recommendation but with stronger wording ("is optimal"), adds 'personalized' and explicitly situates it in pediatric sepsis, increasing certainty and refining the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PedSep-D patients had improved survival odds associated with combined methylprednisolone plus intravenous immunoglobulin therapy.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the treatment-associated improved survival odds claim; no corresponding result is present, so the claim appears removed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A computer tool for identification of individual patient membership is provided at www.pedsepsis.pitt.edu.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a practical contribution (a web tool) that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification accuracy reached as high as 79% in distinguishing schizophrenia patients from healthy individuals in the UCLA dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classification accuracy reached as high as 79% in distinguishing schizophrenia patients from healthy individuals using the k-nearest neighbor algorithm.
alignments.rationale: The published claim reports the same 79% accuracy but specifies the algorithm (k-NN), which clarifies the method without changing the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrated high effectiveness with 85% accuracy in estimating the disease vs. control label for a new dataset acquired domestically.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrated effectiveness with 72% accuracy in estimating the patient's label for a new dataset acquired domestically.
alignments.rationale: Both claims refer to performance on a new domestic dataset, but the published accuracy (72%) is substantially lower than the preprint (85%), representing a revision and a tempered confidence in performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a linear SVM on 62 features obtained from MRMR, patients with schizophrenic subtypes were classified with an accuracy of 64%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients with schizophrenic subtypes were classified with an accuracy of 64% using a linear support vector machine on 62 features obtained from MRMR.
alignments.rationale: The claim is effectively identical in content and metrics between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings of this study underscore the utility of MRI and ML algorithms in enhancing the diagnostic process for schizophrenia.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings underscore the utility of MRI and ML algorithms in enhancing the diagnostic process for schizophrenia.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion wording is equivalent between versions; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Paranoid is the most distinguishable subtype of schizophrenia.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or emphasize that 'paranoid' is the most distinguishable subtype; this specific subtype claim appears omitted in the final paper.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The highest Spearman correlation coefficient between neuroimaging features and behavioral assessments was observed between the degree of the postcentral gyrus and mean reaction time in the verbal capacity task (r = 0.49, p = 0.001).
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a new specific neuroimaging–behavior correlation result that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Zero-shot GPT models achieved promising median accuracy rates in seizure freedom classification, with the best performance reaching 74%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Zero-shot GPT models demonstrate promising median accuracy rates in seizure freedom classification, with the best performance reaching 74%.
alignments.rationale: The result is reported with the same metric and wording; no substantive change between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best performing GPT model outperformed the fine-tuned BERT model in sparse contexts, achieving 76% accuracy compared to 67%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best performing GPT model outperforms the fine-tuned BERT model in sparse contexts, achieving 76% accuracy compared to BERT's 67%.
alignments.rationale: The comparative performance claim and numbers are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates the potential of GPTs in extracting clinically relevant information from unstructured EMR text.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates the potential of GPTs in extracting clinically relevant information from unstructured electronic health record text.
alignments.rationale: Wording ('EMR' vs 'electronic health record') is equivalent; the conclusion is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPTs exhibit superiority over task-specific models in contexts with less precise descriptions of epilepsy and seizures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GPTs exhibit superiority over task-specific models in contexts with less precise descriptions of epilepsy and seizures.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim in substance and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simple prompt engineering techniques enhance model accuracy, providing a framework for leveraging EMR data without clinical annotation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simple prompt engineering techniques enhance model accuracy, providing a framework for leveraging electronic health record data without clinical annotation.
alignments.rationale: Only minor terminology changes; the contribution and claim remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ANOVA feature selector combined with the Random Forest classifier achieved an AUC of 0.83±0.01 in the test dataset of COVID-19 patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AUC, sensitivity, and specificity of the CT-based radiomics model using ANOVA feature selector and Random Forest classifier were 0.83 ± 0.01, 0.81, and 0.72, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same AUC but adds sensitivity and specificity metrics, which clarifies and extends the performance reporting without changing the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In RT-PCR-only positive test sets, similar performance results were obtained, indicating no statistically significant difference.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not present a matching claim specifically about RT-PCR-only positive test sets; this preprint claim is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of the Relief feature selector and Random Forest classifier resulted in the highest performance of AUC in the ComBat harmonized dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the ComBat harmonized dataset, the Relief feature selector combined with the Random Forest classifier achieved the highest performance with an AUC of 0.83 ± 0.01, sensitivity of 0.77, and specificity of 0.74.
alignments.rationale: The published claim restates the same result and augments it with additional performance metrics, preserving the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lung CT radiomics features can be utilized for robust prognostic modeling of COVID-19 in large heterogeneous datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lung CT radiomics features can be used for robust prognostic modeling of COVID-19.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the conclusion that CT radiomics can be used for prognostic modeling; wording is slightly condensed but meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CT radiomics-based models have significant potential for use in prospective clinical settings to improve the management of COVID-19 patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive power of the proposed CT radiomics model is more reliable when using a large multicentric heterogeneous dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the broad clinical-potential claim to a more specific statement about reliability contingent on large multicentric heterogeneous data, narrowing scope and reducing certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: ComBat harmonization did not depict statistically significant improvement compared to a non-harmonized dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit conclusion about the (lack of) benefit from ComBat harmonization that was not present in the preprint's listed claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best model achieved good detection performance for all symptoms, including depression, anxiety, insomnia, and fatigue.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best model achieved good detection performance for depression, anxiety, insomnia, and fatigue, with area under the curve (AUC) values ranging from 0.68 to 0.78.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides numerical AUC ranges that clarify the preprint's qualitative 'good detection' statement without changing its scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individual symptom scores were predicted with good accuracy, with significant correlations between 0.31 and 0.49.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individual symptom scores were accurately predicted, with significant correlations between 0.31 and 0.49.
alignments.rationale: The correlation ranges and conclusion are the same in both versions; wording is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fairness analysis revealed that models were consistent for sex but less so for education level and age groups.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fairness analysis revealed that models were consistent for sex but showed greater disparity for education level and age groups.
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey the same fairness finding (consistency by sex but disparities by education/age); differences are lexical rather than substantive.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates the potential of speech-based systems for multifaceted mental health assessment in the general population.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates the potential of speech-based systems for multifaceted mental health assessment in the general population.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Addressing fairness and incorporating uncertainty estimation with selective classification are key contributions that can enhance the clinical utility of speech-based systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Addressing fairness and incorporating uncertainty estimation with selective classification are key contributions that can enhance the clinical utility of speech-based systems.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is preserved verbatim; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rapid Meta-Analysis (RMA) balances quick time-to-production with reasonable data quality assurances. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel approach to meta-analysis using AI can generate meaningful clinical insights in a much shorter time period than traditional meta-analysis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the RMA specifically as an AI-enabled, faster meta-analysis and emphasizes rapid meaningful insights; this clarifies the preprint's claim about speed/quality balance without directly contradicting it.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RMA identified 11 studies looking at ocular toxicity as a side effect of hydroxychloroquine therapy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RMA identified 11 studies considering ocular toxicity as a side effect of hydroxychloroquine and estimated the incidence to be 3.4% (95% CI 1.11%-9.96%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same identification of 11 studies and includes the identical summary estimate, matching the preprint claim directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The estimated incidence of ocular toxicity as a side effect of hydroxychloroquine therapy is 3.4% (95% CI: 1.11-9.96%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RMA identified 11 studies considering ocular toxicity as a side effect of hydroxychloroquine and estimated the incidence to be 3.4% (95% CI 1.11%-9.96%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same incidence estimate and confidence interval, so the result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The heterogeneity across the individual study findings was high, and interpretation of the result should take this into account. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The heterogeneity across individual study findings was high, which should be taken into account in interpretation of the result. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same limitation about high heterogeneity and the need for cautious interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RMA process, from search to screen to analysis, took less than 30 minutes to produce. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Rapid meta-analysis (RMA) can generate meaningful clinical insights in less than 30 minutes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the RMA can produce results in under 30 minutes; the publication pairs this speed with meaningful insights but preserves the time claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The existing COVID-19 medical literature has significant gaps that remain unidentified and unaddressed as of May 2020. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are still knowledge gaps yet to be filled and areas for improvement for the global research community. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the existence of knowledge gaps and areas needing improvement, matching the preprint's high-level conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The majority of the examined COVID-19 literature focuses on clinical features and diagnosis of the disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The early COVID-19 medical literature originated primarily from Asia and focused mainly on clinical features and diagnosis of the disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds geographic origin detail (Asia) while retaining the preprint's point that early literature focused on clinical features and diagnosis, thus clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many areas of potential research on COVID-19, such as mental health, novel technologies, and indirect effects on non-COVID-19 patients, remain underexplored. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many areas of potential research remain underexplored, such as mental health, the use of novel technologies and artificial intelligence, pathophysiology of COVID-19 within different body systems, and indirect effects of COVID-19 on the care of non-COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication lists similar underexplored areas but with more detail and a higher stated certainty, thus clarifying and amplifying the preprint's claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: International research collaboration on COVID-19 was limited, which may hinder global containment efforts. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Few articles involved research collaboration at the international level (24.7%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a numeric descriptive finding about limited international collaboration but omits the preprint's causal inference about hindering containment, narrowing and removing the causal interpretation and thus tempering the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model trained on survey responses can predict lifetime suicidal ideation with an AUC of 0.79 for the 96 feature model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 96 feature model for lifetime suicidal ideation achieved an Area under the receiver operating curve (AUC) of 0.79. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same AUC for the 96-feature model, matching the preprint result directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model outperformed random forest classifiers in predicting suicidal ideation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not report or emphasize a comparison to random forest classifiers, so the specific performance comparison appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study serves as a proof-of-concept for using deep learning to improve the prediction of suicidal ideation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates that deep learning can be used to improve the identification of suicidal ideation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication delivers the same proof-of-concept conclusion but with stronger wording and higher stated certainty, thus amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further study is required to ensure the clinical relevance and sample generalizability of the deep learning model. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not explicitly restate this limitation about clinical relevance or generalizability, so the cautionary point appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Coexpression of Cancer Stem Cell (CSC) and Epithelial-to-Mesenchymal Transition (EMT) markers such as OCT4 and ZEB1 are indicative of poor outcome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Coexpression of cancer stem cell (CSC) and epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition (EMT) markers such as OCT4 and ZEB1 are indicative of poor outcome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OCT4 and the immune markers CD8, CD34 and CD163 significantly stratified patients at intermediate Leibovich Score (LS). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: OCT4 and the immune markers CD8, CD34, and CD163 significantly stratified patients at intermediate Leibovich score (LS). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The finding is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Augmenting the Leibovich Score with OCT4 and CD34 improved patient stratification by outcome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Augmenting the Leibovich score with OCT4 and CD34 improved patient stratification by outcome. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the result but reports it with lower stated certainty, so the claim's confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining molecular markers has prognostic value and can be integrated with morphological features to improve risk stratification and personalised therapy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining molecular markers has prognostic value and can be integrated with morphological features to improve risk stratification and personalised therapy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GeoMx ® DSP and AI image analysis are complementary tools providing high multiplexing capability required to investigate the Tumour Microenvironment (TME) of ccRCC, while reducing observer bias. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GeoMx® DSP and AI image analysis are complementary tools providing high multiplexing capability required to investigate the TME of ccRCC, while reducing observer bias. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this methodological/contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deploying a conversational artificial intelligence solution is associated with improved recovery rates in mental health services. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deploying the conversational AI solution Limbic Access is associated with improved recovery rates in mental health services. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication names the specific solution (Limbic Access) but maintains the same association between deployment and improved recovery rates.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IAPT services that introduced the conversational AI solution improved their recovery rates, while comparable services reported deteriorating recovery rates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NHS Talking Therapies services that introduced the conversational AI solution improved their recovery rates, while comparable services reported deteriorating recovery rates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the specific service context (NHS Talking Therapies) but otherwise retains the comparative result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The usage of the AI tool can be highly cost-effective relative to other methods of improving recovery rates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The usage of the AI solution Limbic Access can be highly cost-effective relative to other methods of improving recovery rates. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the cost-effectiveness claim but reports it with lower stated certainty (medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI solutions have the potential to support mental health services in delivering quality care amidst workforce shortages and system overburdening. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is an urgent need for digital support to facilitate access to mental healthcare due to the overburdening of mental health services. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the point as an urgent need for digital support (broader policy framing) and states it with higher certainty, thereby clarifying and amplifying the preprint's more tentative conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep Visual Proteomics (DVP) identifies DMBT1, MARCKS, and CD99 as potential markers of risk stratification correlated with disease recurrence history.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DVP identified DMBT1, MARCKS, and CD99 as protein markers linked to recurrence, suggesting their potential for risk assessment.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same marker set and link to recurrence but softens the certainty phrasing ("suggesting their potential") relative to the preprint, reducing confidence while keeping scope constant.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DVP uncovered a metabolic switch towards anaerobic glycolysis in areas of high dysplasia, specific for cells with high CDX2 expression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DVP detected a metabolic shift to anaerobic glycolysis in cells with high CDX2 expression.
alignments.rationale: Both describe a metabolic shift linked to high CDX2 expression, but the publication omits the explicit "areas of high dysplasia" qualifier, effectively broadening the claim to cells with high CDX2 generally.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings underscore the potential of spatial proteomics to refine early-stage detection and contribute to personalized patient management strategies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings underscore the potential of spatial proteomics to refine early stage detection and contribute to personalized patient management strategies.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same concluding statement but with stronger expressed certainty (publication marked high vs preprint medium), increasing confidence while leaving content and scope the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generative AI model successfully transformed MRI images of healthy individuals into images resembling those of patients with schizophrenia.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generative AI model successfully transformed MRI images of healthy individuals into images resembling those of patients with schizophrenia.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim with the same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model identified brain volume changes consistent with existing case-control studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model identified brain volume changes consistent with existing case-control studies.
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced in the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can simulate disease comorbidities and disease progression while preserving individual characteristics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can simulate disease comorbidities and disease progression while preserving individual characteristics.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same simulation capabilities with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings indicate that the generative AI model can capture subtle changes in brain structures associated with schizophrenia.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generative AI model provides a novel tool for visualizing brain changes in different diseases.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from capturing schizophrenia-specific subtle structural changes to a broader visualization tool for multiple diseases, expanding scope while keeping similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The potential of this model extends to advancements in the simulation of disease mechanisms, contributing to the refinement of therapeutic strategies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The potential of this model may contribute to the refinement of therapeutic strategies.
alignments.rationale: Both claims link the model to therapeutic strategy refinement; the publication uses slightly more cautious wording ("may contribute") but preserves the general conclusion and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a substantial shortage of suitable studies comparing healthcare professionals with and without ADM systems regarding patient-relevant endpoints.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A substantial shortage of suitable studies comparing healthcare professionals with and without algorithmic decision-making systems is expected.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the shortage of suitable comparative studies using similar wording and certainty; terminology differs slightly (ADM vs algorithmic decision-making) but meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prioritization of technical quality criteria and clinical parameters over patient-relevant endpoints in study designs contributes to the shortage of suitable studies.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include or discuss the causal contribution of study design prioritization to the shortage; this specific explanatory claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A significant portion of the identified studies is expected to exhibit relatively poor methodological quality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many identified studies are anticipated to exhibit relatively poor methodological quality and provide limited generalisable results.
alignments.rationale: The publication combines the point about poor methodological quality with limited generalisability, expanding the claim slightly while preserving the assessment of methodological weakness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The studies that are identified are anticipated to provide only limited generalizable results.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many identified studies are anticipated to exhibit relatively poor methodological quality and provide limited generalisable results.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states the limited generalisability alongside methodological concerns, matching the preprint's claim about limited generalizable results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.89 in the UK Biobank internal validation dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.89 in the UK Biobank internal validation dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the AUROC result verbatim with identical scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrated a sensitivity of 84% and specificity of 90% for detecting individuals with elevated ASCVD risk scores in the UK Biobank dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrated a sensitivity of 84% and specificity of 90% for detecting individuals with elevated ASCVD risk scores in the UK Biobank dataset.
alignments.rationale: The sensitivity and specificity results are reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the EyePACS 10K dataset, the model achieved a sensitivity of 94% and specificity of 72% with the addition of a regression-derived diabetes modifier.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the EyePACS 10K dataset, the model achieved a sensitivity of 94% and specificity of 72% with the addition of a regression-derived diabetes modifier.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this dataset-specific performance claim exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates that deep learning models using retinal images can provide an additional approach to estimating ASCVD risk.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates that deep learning models using retinal images can provide an additional approach to estimating ASCVD risk.
alignments.rationale: The concluding statement is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning-based CT diagnosis system (DeepPneumonia) can accurately identify COVID-19 patients with an AUC of 0.99 and a recall of 0.93.
alignments.pub_claim_text: When integrating three types of CT images, the model achieved a recall of 0.93 with precision of 0.86 for discriminating COVID-19 patients from others.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a recall of 0.93 but ties it specifically to integrating three types of CT images and omits the AUC=0.99; thus the result is revised and presented in a more conditional context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can discriminate between COVID-19 infected patients and bacteria pneumonia-infected patients with an AUC of 0.95 and a recall of 0.96.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model could accurately discriminate COVID-19 patients from bacterial pneumonia patients with an AUC of 0.95, recall of 0.96, and precision of 0.79.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the discrimination performance and adds a precision metric, clarifying the performance profile without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model is capable of localizing main lesion features, particularly ground-glass opacity (GGO), which assists doctors in diagnosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model could extract main lesion features, especially the ground-glass opacity (GGO), which are visually helpful for assisted diagnoses by doctors.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claim that the model extracts lesion features (GGO) that visually assist diagnosis, matching the preprint's content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The diagnosis for a patient can be completed in 30 seconds using the developed system.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not include the specific operational time-per-diagnosis (30 seconds); this practical performance detail is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An online server is available for online diagnoses with CT images.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention availability of an online server for diagnoses, so this contribution is absent from the final claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of AssistDent significantly improves third-year dental students' ability to detect enamel-only proximal caries in bitewing radiographs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AssistDent AI software significantly improves dentists' ability to detect enamel-only proximal caries.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same improvement but refers to 'dentists' rather than 'third-year dental students', which broadens the target population and thus revises the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Students using AssistDent demonstrated a mean sensitivity level of 0.80, compared to 0.50 for those not using the software.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental group found 75.8% of the caries, compared to 44.3% found by the control group.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents updated/precise percentages that correspond to the preprint sensitivity numbers (rounded/expressed differently), clarifying the reported performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The improvement in detection ability with AssistDent was achieved without an increase in false positives.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The increase in sensitivity of 71% and decrease in specificity of 11% are statistically significant (p <0.01).
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly reports a decrease in specificity (i.e., more false positives), which contradicts the preprint claim of no increase in false positives and thus revises it.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AssistDent could be considered a tool to support minimum intervention and preventive dentistry in teaching hospitals and general practice.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim presents the recommended clinical/teaching adoption or statement about supporting minimum intervention/preventive dentistry, so this conclusion appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher proportion of epithelial tissue was associated with increased breast cancer risk.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A higher proportion of epithelial tissue was associated with increased breast cancer risk.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same association without substantive change in wording or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BBD subtypes, parity, and number of births were differentially associated with all three tissue regions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Direction of associations between benign breast disease subtypes, parity, and number of births with breast composition varied by tissue region.
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases but conveys the same finding that associations vary by tissue region.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No morphometric signature was associated with breast cancer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: No morphometric signature was associated with breast cancer.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim present in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The amount of epithelial tissue may be incorporated into risk assessment models to improve breast cancer risk prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The amount of epithelial tissue may be incorporated into risk assessment models to improve breast cancer risk prediction.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same recommendation but with higher stated certainty (preprint medium → publication high), indicating amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system presented achieved a classification performance of F1 = 0.63.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classification performance of the system was F1 = 0.63.
alignments.rationale: Exact performance figure is reproduced in the publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The span detection performance of the system was F1 = 0.44.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not report the span detection F1=0.44 result, so this specific metric claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The end-to-end entity resolution performance of the system was F1 = 0.34.
alignments.rationale: The publication's summarised claims do not include this end-to-end F1 figure, so the specific claim is not present in the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mining ADEs from Twitter posts using a pipeline architecture requires the different components to be trained and tuned based on input data imbalance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mining ADEs from Twitter posts using a pipeline architecture requires the different components to be trained and tuned based on input data imbalance.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this methodological conclusion verbatim, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean Dice coefficients for lung and lesions segmentation were 0.98±0.011 and 0.91±0.038, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The COLI-Net deep learning model achieved a mean Dice coefficient of 0.98 for lung segmentation and 0.91 for COVID-19 lesions segmentation.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same Dice coefficients for lung and lesion segmentation, matching the preprint's numeric results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean relative Hounsfield unit differences for lung and lesions were 0.03±0.84% and −0.18±3.4%, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean relative Hounsfield unit differences for lung and lesions were 0.03% and -0.18%, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same HU difference results, dropping the ± uncertainty in the headline claim and thus presenting a slightly clarified summary.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The relative volume difference for lung and lesions were 0.38±1.2% and 0.81±6.6%, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The relative volume difference for lung and lesions were 0.38% and 0.81%, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same relative volume differences without the ± uncertainty values in the succinct claim, clarifying the presentation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most radiomic features had a mean relative error less than 5%, with the highest mean relative error for the lung being -6.95%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most radiomic features had a mean relative error of less than 5%, with the highest mean relative error for the lung range feature at -6.95% and the least axis length shape feature for lesions at 8.68%.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides additional detail about specific features (and an extra lesion feature error), expanding and clarifying the preprint summary.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An automated deep learning-guided framework for lung and pneumonia lesion detection and quantification was developed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed automated DL-guided segmentation framework provides a fast, consistent, robust, and human error immune method for lung and pneumonia lesion detection and quantification.
alignments.rationale: The publication elaborates on the qualities and claimed benefits (fast, robust, human error immune) of the developed framework, amplifying the conclusion from mere development to performance/benefit claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models yielded mean DSCs from 0.71 (ALL) to 0.73 (T2+T1).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning using multiparametric MRI provides reasonably accurate segmentations of oropharyngeal cancer primary gross tumor volume that may be comparable to ground truth segmentations generated by clinical experts.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the numeric segmentation performance as a general conclusion about reasonable accuracy; the specific DSC figures are summarized into a broader claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Performance was significantly improved for HD, FND, sensitivity, surface DSC, and 95% HD for the T2+T1 model compared to the T2 model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating additional multiparametric MRI channels may increase the performance of false-negative DSC, sensitivity, surface DSC, Hausdorff distance, and 95% Hausdorff distance.
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserted statistically significant improvements; the publication adopts more cautious language ('may increase'), tempering the strength of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There were no differences between ground truth and DL-generated segmentations for all observers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: On average, there were no significant differences between ground truth and deep learning-generated segmentations for all observers.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same finding that, on average, there were no significant differences between ground truth and model segmentations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DL using mpMRI provides high-quality segmentations of OPC GTVp.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning using multiparametric MRI provides reasonably accurate segmentations of oropharyngeal cancer primary gross tumor volume that may be comparable to ground truth segmentations generated by clinical experts.
alignments.rationale: The publication tones the conclusion to 'reasonably accurate' and 'may be comparable', which is a more cautious phrasing than the preprint's stronger 'high-quality' claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean test set Dice similarity coefficient (DSC) of the 3D model was 0.96 and the 2D model was 0.95. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean test set Dice similarity coefficient (DSC) of the 3D and 2D models were 0.96 and 0.95, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric DSC values and their model assignments are identical between preprint and publication with only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predicted skeletal muscle index (SMI) had a high correlation to the ground-truth SMI in both males and females (r>0.96). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predicted skeletal muscle index (SMI) had high correlation to the ground-truth SMI in males and females (r>0.96). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The assertion, correlation value, and populations are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predicted SMI stratified patients for overall survival in males (log-rank p = 0.01) but not in females (log-rank p = 0.07). (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predicted SMI stratified patients for overall survival in males (log-rank p = 0.01) but not females (log-rank p = 0.07). (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The survival stratification results, p-values, and sex-specific finding are the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A high-performance, multi-stage, fully-automated approach to segment cervical vertebra skeletal muscle was developed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed a high-performance, multi-stage, fully-automated approach to segment cervical vertebra skeletal muscle. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both texts state the same development of a high-performance multi-stage automated segmentation approach with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study represents an essential step towards fully-automated sarcopenia-related decision-making. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our study is an essential step towards fully-automated sarcopenia-related decision-making in patients with head and neck cancer. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the patient population (head and neck cancer), narrowing the scope while preserving the original claim about progress toward automated sarcopenia decision-making.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Each one-year increase in retinal age gap is associated with increased arterial stiffness index (ASI). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Each 1-year increase in retinal age gap was associated with increased arterial stiffness index. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The association and direction are identical between preprint and publication with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Each one-year increase in retinal age gap is associated with a 3% increase in the risk of incident cardiovascular disease (CVD). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Each 1-year increase in retinal age gap was associated with a 3% increase in the risk of incident cardiovascular disease (CVD). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported effect size and interpretation are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The risk of incident CVD increases significantly when retinal age gap reaches 1.21. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The risk of incident CVD increased significantly when retinal age gap reached 1.21. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical threshold and finding are unchanged, but the publication reports the claim with higher certainty than the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Retinal age gap is significantly associated with ASI and incident CVD events. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Retinal age gap was significantly associated with arterial stiffness index and incident CVD events. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion summarizing the two primary associations is the same across both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The morphological characteristics of ecHFOs include lower amplitude throughout the 80-500 Hz frequency band around the HFO onset compared to non-ecHFOs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ecHFOs had lower amplitude throughout the 80-500 Hz frequency band around the HFO onset compared to non-ecHFOs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The morphological comparison and frequency band are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A minority of ecHFOs were identified as HFOs with spikes, accounting for 22.9% of the total. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A minority of ecHFOs were HFOs with spikes, accounting for 22.9% of the total. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The proportion and characterization are the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction of postoperative seizure outcomes improved when using the resection ratio of non-ecHFOs, with an area under the ROC curve of 0.82, increased from 0.76. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using the resection ratio of non-ecHFOs improved the prediction of postoperative seizure outcomes compared to using uncorrected HFOs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the improvement as a direct comparison to uncorrected HFOs rather than reporting the specific AUC numbers; the substantive result is the same but slightly rephrased/clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study suggests that the DL-based HFO classification might help separate physiological from pathological HFOs and guide surgical resection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DL-based HFO classification might help separate physiological from pathological HFOs and guide surgical resection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about potential clinical utility of the DL classification is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep learning approach was developed for prognostic models of malignant transformation in oral epithelial dysplasia (OED). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep learning approach was developed for prognostic models of malignant transformation in oral epithelial dysplasia (OED). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the same methodological contribution without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average AUROC of ∼0.78 was achieved for predicting malignant transformations in OED using stratified 5-fold cross-validation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The weakly supervised method achieved an average area under the receiver-operator characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.78 for predicting malignant transformation in OED. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported AUROC and performance claim are equivalent; the publication clarifies the method label but not the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Significant prognostic factors for malignant transformation include the count of peri-epithelial lymphocytes, epithelial layer nuclei count, and basal layer nuclei count, all with p < 0.05. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Significant prognostic factors for malignant transformation include the count of peri-epithelial lymphocytes, epithelial layer nuclei count, and basal layer nuclei count, all with p-values less than 0.05. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The list of significant factors and their statistical significance are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Progression free survival is associated with epithelial layer nuclei count, basal layer nuclei count, and peri-epithelial lymphocyte count, with C-index values indicating a high risk of malignant transformation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Progression-free survival (PFS) was associated with epithelial layer nuclei count, basal layer nuclei count, and peri-epithelial lymphocytes count, with C-index values indicating their predictive capability. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The association between features and PFS is preserved, but the publication frames the C-index as predictive capability and lowers certainty from high to medium, tempering the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of deep learning for prognostication and progression free survival prediction of OED has significant potential to aid patient management. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's concluding statement about clinical potential does not appear in the publication; instead the paper adds a limitation (new) rather than repeating this conclusion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Further evaluation and testing on multi-centre data is required for validation and translation to clinical practice. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This limitation/requirement for external validation was not present in the preprint and is newly added in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: hART achieves an area under the precision-recall of 28% for HF risk prediction, which is a 33% improvement over existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: hART achieves an area under the precision-recall of 28% for HF risk prediction, which is a 33% improvement over existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported performance metric and comparative improvement are identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with severe CHD lesions showed a consistently elevated predicted HF risks throughout their lifespan. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients with severe CHD lesions showed a consistently elevated predicted HF risks throughout their lifespan. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The finding about lifelong elevated predicted HF risk in severe CHD patients is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The timing of interventions such as arrhythmia surgery had varying impacts on the lifespan HF risk among individuals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The timing of interventions such as arrhythmia surgery had varying impacts on the lifespan HF risk among individuals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The statement about timing of interventions and variable impacts is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Arrhythmic surgery performed at a younger age had minimal long-term effects on HF risk, while surgeries during adulthood had a significant lasting impact. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the specific age-stratified effects of arrhythmic surgery described in the preprint; it reports a more general statement about timing instead.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: hART can detect meaningful lifelong HF risk in CHD patients by capturing both long and short-range dependencies in their past medical events. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: hART can detect meaningful lifelong HF risk in CHD patients by capturing both long and short-range dependencies in their past medical events. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The methodological claim about hART's capability and rationale is the same in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GBT model achieved the best AUC-ROC scores of 0.809, 0.821, 0.822, 0.808, and 0.833 for 1-year, 2-year, 3-year, 4-year, and 5-year prediction windows, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GBT model achieved the best AUC-ROC scores of 0.809-0.833 across 1- to 5-year prediction windows.
alignments.rationale: The numerical AUC results are the same and the publication compresses the range into a summary format without changing meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SHAP analysis highlighted key risk factors for ADRD, including depressive disorder, heart disease, higher age, headache, anxiety, and insomnia.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SHAP analysis identified depressive disorder, age groups 80-90 yrs and 70-80 yrs, heart disease, anxiety, and the novel risk factors of sleep apnea and headache.
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the list (adds sleep apnea, specifies age groups, omits insomnia) and frames some factors as novel with higher certainty, altering the original feature set and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates the potential of ML models using EHR data for early ADRD prediction, enabling timely interventions to delay progression and improve outcomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study underscores the potential of ML models for leveraging EHR data to enable early ADRD prediction.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core claim about potential for early prediction but shortens and tones down the downstream intervention/impact language and is assigned lower certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings offer new opportunities for personalized screening and management strategies, advancing both clinical and informatics science.
alignments.rationale: This conclusion about personalized screening and informatics advances appears in the publication but is not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RF model can predict patient outcomes with a sensitivity of 70% and a specificity of 75%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RF model can predict patient outcomes with a sensitivity of 70% and a specificity of 75%.
alignments.rationale: The published result repeats the identical performance metrics and claim from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fifteen factors with the highest predictive values for mortality in COVID-19 ICU patients were identified, including gender, age, BUN, creatinine, INR, and albumin.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fifteen factors with the highest predictive values for mortality in COVID-19 ICU patients were identified, including gender, age, BUN, creatinine, INR, and albumin.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same set of identified factors without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increased levels of BUN, lowered albumin levels, increased creatinine, INR, and RDW, along with gender and age, were the most decisive variables in the predictive model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increased levels of BUN, lowered albumin levels, increased creatinine, INR, and RDW, along with gender and age, were the most decisive variables in the model.
alignments.rationale: The variables and their role in the model are stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can be a useful tool in COVID-19 ICU decision-making, emphasizing the importance of early triage decisions.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the preprint's operational/utility claim about ICU decision-making and early triage; instead it adds a different general high-accuracy conclusion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine-learning-based model designed using two independent patient datasets can predict the risk of mortality from severe COVID-19 with high accuracy.
alignments.rationale: This high-level statement about model design using two datasets and high accuracy is present in the publication but was not in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ratio of two distinct myeloid dendritic cell subsets at baseline predicts immune response to a single dose of the hepatitis B virus vaccine.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ratio of two distinct myeloid dendritic cell subsets at baseline correlates with the immune response to a single dose of the hepatitis B virus vaccine.
alignments.rationale: The publication softens 'predicts' to 'correlates' but otherwise retains the same finding about the baseline ratio and single-dose response.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Participants in the vaccine study were in one of two different dendritic cell dispositional states at baseline, which influenced their antibody response to the vaccine.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants in the vaccine study were in one of two different dendritic cell dispositional states at baseline, which influenced their antibody response to the vaccine.
alignments.rationale: The disposition-state finding is carried forward verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictive model constructed using genes expressed in myeloid dendritic cell subsets showed an improved ability to predict serum antibody titers in response to vaccination.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictive models constructed using genes expressed in myeloid dendritic cell subsets showed improved correlation with serum antibody titers in response to full vaccination.
alignments.rationale: The publication modifies the claim from 'ability to predict' to 'improved correlation' and refers to full vaccination rather than (single-dose) vaccination, changing both scope and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dispositional endotype of circulating dendritic cells could dictate response to vaccination.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dispositional endotype of circulating dendritic cells at pre-vaccination baseline could dictate the response to vaccination.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conceptual conclusion about dispositional endotypes determining vaccine response.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Modulating myeloid dendritic cells via TLR stimulation could guide the design of effective single dose vaccination strategies.
alignments.rationale: The recommendation regarding TLR stimulation to guide single-dose vaccine strategy does not appear in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Piacenza score, a machine-learning based score, predicts 30-day mortality in patients with COVID-19 pneumonia.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A customizable machine learning-based score with a purely data-driven selection of features is feasible and effective for the prediction of mortality among patients with COVID-19 pneumonia.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same overall conclusion about the score's predictive utility and feasibility, matching the preprint's assertion that it predicts 30-day mortality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Piacenza score achieved an AUC of 0.78 in the internal validation cohort and 0.79 in the external validation cohort.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Piacenza score exhibited an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) of 0.78 in the internal validation cohort and 0.79 in the external validation cohort.
alignments.rationale: The numerical AUC results are reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of the Piacenza score is comparable to that of the 4C score and a Naïve Bayes model with a-priori chosen features.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Piacenza score shows comparable accuracy with respect to the 4C score and a naïve Bayes model with a priori chosen features.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the direct comparison and comparable accuracy with the same benchmark models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A personalized machine-learning based score with purely data-driven feature selection is feasible and effective for predicting mortality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A customizable machine learning-based score with a purely data-driven selection of features is feasible and effective for the prediction of mortality among patients with COVID-19 pneumonia.
alignments.rationale: The publication's conclusion about feasibility and effectiveness of a data-driven ML score matches the preprint's statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Gradient Boosting Regression model performed best, resulting in a ML-BA with R-squared value of 0.270 and MAE of 6.519.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Gradient Boosting Regression model produced an ML-BA with an R-squared value of 0.270 and a mean absolute error of 6.519.
alignments.rationale: The reported performance metrics and model selection are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ML-BA was significantly associated with disability in basic activities of daily living, instrumental activities of daily living, lower extremity mobility, and upper extremity mobility, and mortality, with odds ratios ranging from 1% to 7% per one-year increment in ML-BA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ML-BA was significantly associated with disability in basic activities of daily living, instrumental activities of daily living, lower extremity mobility, and upper extremity mobility, as well as mortality.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the multivariate association with disabilities and mortality though it omits the specific per-year odds-range in the summary claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These associations were generally comparable to that of KDM-BA.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the comparative statement to KDM-BA in the provided claims, so this preprint comparison appears to be omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study provides a valid ML-based measure of biological aging for middle-aged and older Chinese adults.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study provides a valid ML-based measure of biological aging for middle-aged and older Chinese adults.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim about a valid ML-based biological age measure is carried forward unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings support the application of ML in geroscience research and help facilitate the understanding of the aging process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings support the application of machine learning in geroscience research.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves support for ML in geroscience but omits the explicit phrasing about facilitating understanding of aging, narrowing the scope of the original claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The associations of the ML-BA with disability and mortality were independent of chronological age.
alignments.rationale: The independence-from-chronological-age conclusion appears in the publication but is not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A total of seven COVID-19 clinical profiles were identified, which were characterized by a specific composition of symptoms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Seven clinical profiles with specific compositions of clinical manifestations were identified using symptom-based clustering.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result (seven symptom-based clinical profiles) with equivalent wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A lower viral load was found for the asymptomatic group compared to other clusters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A lower viral load was found for the asymptomatic group compared to other clusters.
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same, but the publication reports a lower certainty level (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The host co-morbidities and the SARS-CoV-2 genotypes are not specific to a particular profile, rather they are present in all the groups, including asymptomatic cases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Host co-morbidities and SARS-CoV-2 genotypes are not specific to a particular clinical profile, as they are present in all groups, including asymptomatic cases.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion but with higher stated certainty, increasing confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A total of 18 symptoms at the time of diagnosis of SARS-CoV-2 infection were reported with a frequency greater than 1%.
alignments.rationale: This descriptive symptom-frequency result appears only in the publication and was not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings can be used for decision-making by local healthcare institutions regarding case definition and infrastructure.
alignments.rationale: This application/implication statement is new in the publication and was not in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a predictive relationship between depression and chronic pain interference.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a predictive relationship between depression and chronic pain interference.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same primary result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High perceived stress, low pain self-efficacy, and poor sleep quality mediate the relationship between depression and chronic pain interference.
alignments.pub_claim_text: High perceived stress, low pain self-efficacy, and poor sleep quality mediate the relationship between depression and chronic pain interference.
alignments.rationale: Identical mediation finding reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrative Medical Group Visits (IMGV) can be used as adjuncts to medications for treating chronic pain in underserved populations.
alignments.rationale: The specific recommendation about IMGV as adjuncts does not appear in the publication and thus was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings may inform future treatment optimization and targeting in racially diverse, low income populations suffering from chronic pain and depression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings may inform future treatment optimization and targeting in racialized, income-disadvantaged populations.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion but uses slightly different wording to describe the target populations (clarified phrasing).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The estimated mean recovery period for COVID-19 is 22.14 days with a 95% Confidence Interval of 22.00 to 22.27.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The estimated mean recovery period for COVID-19 is 22.14 days with a 95% Confidence Interval of 22.00–22.27.
alignments.rationale: Exact same numerical result and confidence interval in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The bimodal gamma distribution indicates the presence of two groups of recovered individuals: one with a short recovery period of mean 21.02 days and another with a long recovery period of mean 38.88 days.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The bimodal gamma distribution indicates the presence of two groups of recovered individuals: one with a short recovery period (mean 21.02 days) and another with a long recovery period (mean 38.88 days).
alignments.rationale: Publication repeats the same bimodal recovery-group finding with identical values.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The characteristics of the decease period and the recovery period are alike.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The characteristics of the decease period and the recovery period are similar.
alignments.rationale: Same conclusion presented with synonymous wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A high probability domain for recovered individuals is observed with respect to incubation and recovery periods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A high probability domain for recovered individuals is observed with respect to incubation and recovery periods.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this probabilistic observation unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A similar high probability domain is obtained for deaths when analyzing the bivariate distribution of incubation and decease periods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A similar high probability domain is obtained for deaths when analyzing the bivariate distribution of incubation and decease periods.
alignments.rationale: Identical result carried through to the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The elastic-net model has good performance with an AUC of 0.876, sensitivity of 0.772, and specificity of 0.803 for predicting cognitive impairment (FSIQ<80).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The elastic-net model demonstrates good performance in predicting cognitive impairment in children with autism, achieving an AUC of 0.876.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core performance claim (AUC) but omits sensitivity/specificity details, narrowing the reported metrics and clarifying emphasis on AUC.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Top ranked predictive features for cognitive impairment included parent-reported language and cognitive levels, age at autism diagnosis, and history of services.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Top-ranked predictive features for cognitive impairment include parent-reported language and cognitive levels, age at autism diagnosis, and history of services.
alignments.rationale: Feature ranking is reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cognitive levels can be imputed with high accuracy for children with autism using commonly collected parent-reported data and standardized surveys.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cognitive levels can be imputed with high accuracy for children with autism using commonly collected parent-reported data and standardized surveys.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this conclusion verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The current model provides a method for large scale autism studies to estimate cognitive ability when standardized psychometric testing is not feasible.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The current model offers a method for large-scale autism studies to estimate cognitive ability when standardized psychometric testing is not feasible.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same contribution but rates its certainty slightly lower (contribution certainty medium vs preprint high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning can correctly predict cognitive impairment about 80% of the time when standardized cognitive testing scores are missing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The elastic-net model demonstrates good performance in predicting cognitive impairment in children with autism, achieving an AUC of 0.876.
alignments.rationale: The informal 'about 80%' accuracy claim from the preprint is represented in the publication by a different performance metric (AUC 0.876) rather than an explicit ~80% accuracy, so the claim was revised and narrowed to AUC.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ML model developed demonstrated no substantial differences between the observed and predicted mean salt intake.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ML model demonstrated no substantial differences between the observed and predicted mean salt intake (p<0.001).
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the 'no substantial differences' conclusion but adds statistical reporting (p-value) that complicates interpretation; this clarifies wording but appears to temper confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall predicted mean salt consumption across the pooled dataset was 8.1 g/day.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predicted mean salt consumption ranged from 6.8 g/day in Eritrea to 10.0 g/day in American Samoa.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes country-level ranges rather than a single pooled mean, broadening the presentation and changing the specific summary metric.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ML model could be used to predict mean salt consumption in populations where urine samples are not available.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ML model could be used to predict mean salt consumption in the general population where urine samples are not available.
alignments.rationale: Same intended application is stated in both versions with very similar wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The countries with the highest predicted mean salt intake were in the Western Pacific region.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predicted mean salt consumption ranged from 6.8 g/day in Eritrea to 10.0 g/day in American Samoa.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides country-level results that are consistent with the preprint's statement about higher intake in Western Pacific countries.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The country-specific predicted mean salt intake was within reasonable difference from the best available evidence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The country-specific predicted mean salt intake was within reasonable difference from the best available evidence.
alignments.rationale: This assessment comparing predictions to existing evidence is present and unchanged in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning model was developed to predict salt consumption at the population level based on simple predictors.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly frames the work as development of a population-level predictive ML model, phrased as a contribution not separately stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed machine learning-based analysis tool can successfully identify explicit and implicit knowledge in the pattern identification process of traditional Asian medicine doctors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tool successfully identifies explicit and implicit knowledge in the pattern identification process for eight doctors.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint result but adds the specific scope ('for eight doctors'), which clarifies and narrows the original claim while keeping confidence high.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tool evaluates and compares the decision-making processes of traditional Asian medicine doctors in terms of their explicit and implicit knowledge.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study proposes a machine learning-based analysis tool to evaluate the clinical decision-making process of pattern identification in traditional medicine.
alignments.rationale: Both describe the same evaluation-purpose of the tool for clinical decision-making/pattern identification; the publication frames it as the study proposal but does not change the substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are identifiable differences between doctors' own explicit and implicit knowledge and among different traditional Asian medicine doctors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are many differences between the explicit and implicit importance scores in the study.
alignments.rationale: The publication documents the differences more directly ("many differences between explicit and implicit importance scores"), clarifying and presenting the finding with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed tool would be beneficial for the clinical standardization of traditional Asian medicine and for training interns and residents.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The assessment tool could be broadly used for the standardization of precision medicine, including traditional medicine therapeutics.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes broader use for standardization (precision medicine) but omits the specific claim about training interns/residents, changing the contribution's focus and tempering the original benefit claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Implicit importance is more closely related to explicit importance in prescription than in diagnosis.
alignments.rationale: This result about prescription vs diagnosis relationship is present in the publication but was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The area under the ROC curves for the prediction of subjects in the top quartile of observed disease progression was 0.70 in the 10-year follow-up data.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report this specific AUC result or the 10-year top-quartile prediction metric, so the claim appears omitted.
