alignments.pub_claim_text: Measures from DHTs show promise in tracking neurodegenerative disease progression with increased sensitivity and reduced variability as compared to traditional clinical scores. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the preprint's conclusion about DHT measures' promise.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel framework and methodology to construct composite digital measures of disease progression from high-dimensional DHT datasets is presented. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel framework and methodology to construct composite digital measures of disease progression from high-dimensional DHT datasets is presented. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the claimed contribution without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Score for Emergency Risk Prediction (SERP) outperformed several benchmark scores in predicting 30-day mortality and other mortality-related outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SERP outperformed several established clinical scores in predicting 2-day, 7-day, and 30-day mortality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides more specific time horizons (2-,7-,30-day) while retaining the core claim of outperforming established scores, clarifying and broadening the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SERP achieved a sensitivity of 72.6% and a specificity of 77.8% under a cut-off score of 27. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report these specific sensitivity/specificity values or the cut-off; instead it reports AUCs, so the specific sensitivity/specificity claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SERP demonstrated better prediction performance than existing triage scores while maintaining easy implementation and ease of ascertainment at the emergency department. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SERP maintains easy implementation and ease of ascertainment in the emergency department. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the easy-implementation aspect but separates it from the performance claim (which is reported elsewhere); overall the combined preprint claim is presented with lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tool has the potential to be widely applied and validated in different circumstances and healthcare settings. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Score for Emergency Risk Prediction (SERP) tool provides a simple, early, and accurate estimate of patients' risk of death in the emergency department. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the tool more assertively as providing simple, early, accurate risk estimates (higher certainty) rather than merely potential for wide application; this clarifies and increases expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multiple inflammatory and EC activation biomarkers were associated with mortality in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multiple inflammatory and EC activation biomarkers were associated with mortality in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication text are essentially identical in content and certainty about the association with mortality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dysregulation of specific microRNAs at presentation was specific for poor COVID-19-related outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dysregulation of specific microRNAs at presentation was specific for poor COVID-19-related outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about specific microRNA dysregulation and poor outcomes is preserved verbatim between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integration of multi-omics data identified microRNA and vascular biomarkers prognostic of in-hospital mortality in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integration of multi-omics data identified microRNA and vascular biomarkers prognostic of in-hospital mortality in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The multi-omics conclusion is carried forward without substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Targeted stabilization of the endothelial barrier with Q-Peptide and Slit2-N are novel therapeutic avenues that should be explored in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Vascular stabilizing therapies should be explored as a treatment for endothelial dysfunction in COVID-19. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication drops the specific agents named in the preprint and restates the idea more generally as vascular-stabilizing therapies, changing specificity while keeping the therapeutic suggestion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Markers of endothelial dysfunction at presentation likely reflect systemic vascular dysfunction in critically ill patients and are not specific to SARS-CoV-2 infection. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation about lack of SARS-CoV-2 specificity is not present in the publication’s listed claims and appears to have been omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Exposure of ECs to COVID-19 patient plasma resulted in severity-specific gene expression responses and EC barrier dysfunction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports an experimental result on endothelial cells exposed to patient plasma that was not present among the preprint claims, so this is an added result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synthetic data distillation can fine-tune smaller, open-source large-language models to achieve performance similar to that of larger models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fine-tuning smaller, open-source LLMs can achieve performance comparable to larger models while enabling local hardware deployment or reduced cloud costs. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core claim about comparable performance but explicitly adds deployment/cost benefits, clarifying practical advantages beyond the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fine-tuned smaller Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model outperformed the 8B-Instruct model on all tasks and sometimes exceeded the performance of the larger 70B-Instruct model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 8B-parameter model achieved high accuracy across all tasks and sometimes outperformed the 70B-Instruct teacher model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance result for the 8B model relative to baselines and the 70B teacher without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work demonstrates the potential of synthetic data distillation to enable more scalable and efficient clinical information extraction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work demonstrates synthetic data distillation’s potential for enabling scalable clinical information extraction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about enabling scalable clinical information extraction is directly preserved between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of synthetic data distillation could improve the accuracy and efficiency of patient phenotyping and clinical-trial matching. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The specific applications to patient phenotyping and trial matching do not appear among the publication claims and thus seem omitted in the published version.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Fine-tuning with only the most challenging questions still improved performance, demonstrating the value of targeted training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a result about targeted training on hardest questions that was not present in the preprint claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Results from 3B- and 1B-parameter models showed a clear size-performance tradeoff. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The size-performance tradeoff result for smaller parameter models is new in the publication and was not stated among preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DIAMOND method demonstrates high sensitivity in detecting base-pair resolution methylation patterns from plasma DNA. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new method for detecting long interspersed nuclear element altered methylation in plasma DNA demonstrates powerful correct classification rates for discriminating healthy and tumor plasmas across six types of cancers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the method in terms of classification performance across cancers rather than explicitly emphasizing base-pair resolution sensitivity, shifting emphasis of the original technical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning-based classifiers using DIAMOND show powerful correct classification rates, discriminating healthy and tumor plasmas from six types of cancers with AUC values ranging from 88% to 100%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new method for detecting long interspersed nuclear element altered methylation in plasma DNA demonstrates powerful correct classification rates for discriminating healthy and tumor plasmas across six types of cancers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the strong classification performance across six cancer types; the publication preserves the classifier performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DIAMOND assay can perform copy number alterations (CNA) analysis, which improves cancer detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Detection of Long Interspersed Nuclear Element Altered Methylation ON plasma DNA method can perform copy number alteration analysis that improves cancer detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the claim that the method enables CNA analysis which improves detection, matching the preprint claim directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that the DIAMOND assay could lead to the development of more efficient non-invasive diagnostic tests for all cancer patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research indicates that the developed method could lead to more efficient noninvasive diagnostic tests for all patients with cancer. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The translational conclusion about potential for more efficient noninvasive diagnostics is preserved between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models that include both instrumented gait analysis (IGA) and clinical features are better able to estimate treatment outcomes compared to models that include clinical features alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models that include both instrumented gait analysis (IGA) and clinical features are better able to estimate treatment outcomes than those that include clinical features alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core comparative claim between models with and without IGA is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The IGA model was more accurate than the CLI model for mean stance-phase foot progression outcomes, with a root mean square error (RMSE) of 11 degrees compared to 13 degrees. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The IGA model was more accurate than the CLI model for mean stance-phase foot progression outcomes, with a root mean square error of 11° compared to 13°. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical RMSE comparison is repeated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The IGA model explained more than 1.5 times as much variance in outcomes compared to the CLI model, with R² values of 0.45 and 0.28, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The IGA model explained more than 1.5 times as much variance in outcomes compared to the CLI model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the comparative variance-explained claim (numbers effectively preserved), so this is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The IGA model outperformed the CLI model for every level of treatment complexity, as measured by the number of simultaneous surgeries. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The IGA model outperformed the CLI model for every level of treatment complexity as measured by the number of simultaneous surgeries. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about outperformance across treatment complexity levels is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results suggest that IGA has the potential to be useful in the treatment planning process for ambulatory children diagnosed with cerebral palsy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results indicate that IGA has the potential to be useful in the treatment planning process for ambulatory children diagnosed with cerebral palsy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The translational conclusion about IGA's potential utility in treatment planning is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our models learned successfully from early clinical data and predicted clinical endpoints with high accuracy, the best model achieving AUC-ROC scores of 0.75 to 0.83. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our models learned successfully from early clinical data and predicted clinical endpoints with high accuracy, the best model achieving area under the receiver operating characteristic (AUC-ROC) scores of 0.76 to 0.87. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication updates the reported AUC-ROC range (slightly different values), clarifying performance metrics while keeping the original claim about high accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Younger patient age was associated with an increased risk of receiving intensive care and ventilation, but lower risk of mortality. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Younger patient age was associated with an increased risk of receiving intensive care and ventilation, but lower risk of mortality. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The age-associated risks are stated identically and retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clinical indicators of a patient's oxygen supply and selected laboratory results were most predictive of COVID-19 patient trajectories. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clinical indicators of a patient’s oxygen supply and selected laboratory results, such as blood lactate and creatinine levels, were most predictive of COVID-19 patient trajectories. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds specific laboratory examples (lactate, creatinine), clarifying which tests underpin the original predictive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning can aid in the early identification of COVID-19 patients with a poor prognosis, using EHR data collected during a patient's first presentation at the emergency department. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Among COVID-19 patients, machine learning can aid in the early identification of those with a poor prognosis, using EHR data collected during a patient’s first presentation at ED. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is preserved but the publication reports a lower certainty (medium), indicating a tempered confidence level.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Patient age and measures of oxygenation status during ED stay are primary indicators of poor patient outcomes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This synthesized, higher-level conclusion emphasizing age plus oxygenation as primary indicators is not explicitly stated in the preprint claims and appears as an added take-away in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning (ML) technologies have played a substantial role in addressing the challenges posed by the COVID-19 pandemic.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI-based technologies have played a substantial role in addressing the challenges posed by the COVID-19 pandemic.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level conclusion using 'AI' instead of 'ML' with no substantive change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A total of 128 publications were reviewed, which were classified into three themes based on ML applications: Computational Epidemiology, Early Detection and Diagnosis, and Disease Progression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Of the 130 studies analyzed, 54.6% focused on predicting the COVID-19 outbreak and the impact of containment policies.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the study set with a slightly different total (130) and reports theme distributions as percentages rather than the preprint's statement of 128 and a three-theme classification, making the reporting more precise.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Seventy studies focused on predicting the outbreak and impact of containment policies, which were grouped under the Computational Epidemiology theme.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Of the 130 studies analyzed, 54.6% focused on predicting the COVID-19 outbreak and the impact of containment policies.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same content for this theme but reports it as a percentage of a slightly different total, effectively clarifying the count.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Forty studies applied ML techniques to detect COVID-19 using patients' radiological images or lab results, categorized under Early Detection and Diagnosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 30.8% of the studies applied AI techniques to detect COVID-19 using patients' radiological images or laboratory test results.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same detection-focused theme as a percentage (30.8%) rather than an absolute count (40), aligning with the same content but in different format.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Eighteen publications predicted disease progression and outcomes for COVID-19 positive patients, classified under the Disease Progression theme.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 14.6% of the studies focused on predicting disease progression and outcomes for patients with COVID-19.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same disease-progression finding as a percentage (14.6%) rather than the preprint's absolute count (18), representing a clarified presentation of the same result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An explainable machine learning approach can predict inter-patient specific variation in drug response for inflammatory bowel diseases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An explainable machine learning approach can integrate multi-modal data to predict inter-patient variation in drug response for inflammatory bowel diseases.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the description by explicitly stating integration of multi-modal data but retains the same core conclusion about predicting inter-patient variation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best model achieved a prediction error as low as 4.98% on unseen patients when predicting TNFα levels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best model predicted TNFα levels from demographic, medicinal, and genomic features with an error of only 4.98% on unseen patients.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides the same quantitative performance but clarifies the input feature types used to achieve that error, without changing the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Variations in drug effectiveness were observed between patients based on gender, age, or condition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Variations in drug effectiveness were observed between patients based on gender, age, or condition, linking new genetic polymorphisms to response variation to the anti-inflammatory treatment BIRB796.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the observed demographic/clinical variation but augments the claim by linking genetic polymorphisms; the core observation is the same but more detailed in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New genetic polymorphisms were linked to variation in response to the anti-inflammatory treatment BIRB796 in IBD patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Variations in drug effectiveness were observed between patients based on gender, age, or condition, linking new genetic polymorphisms to response variation to the anti-inflammatory treatment BIRB796.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same combined observation linking genetic polymorphisms to variant drug response, matching the preprint claim closely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A total of 877 characterized biochemicals were identified, with 9% differing significantly between PLWH with and without MetS.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nine percent of the identified biochemicals differed significantly between PLWH with and without MetS.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the key quantitative result (9% differing) but omits the explicit total (877) reported in the preprint, making the presentation more streamlined.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A consensus identification indicates 11 biomarkers of MetS phenotype in PLWH, with seven having higher abundance in PLWH with MetS.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A consensus identification indicated 11 biomarkers of the MetS phenotype in PLWH.
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms the consensus identification of 11 biomarkers but omits the specific detail about seven being higher in abundance, simplifying the reported result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Altered amino acid metabolism is a central characteristic of PLWH with MetS.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Altered glutamate metabolism is associated with MetS in PLWH.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the broader preprint claim about amino acid metabolism to a more specific association with glutamate metabolism, representing a substantive narrowing of scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A potential central role for glutamate metabolism in establishing the MetS phenotype in PLWH is suggested.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Altered glutamate metabolism is associated with MetS in PLWH.
alignments.rationale: The preprint tentatively suggested a central role for glutamate metabolism; the publication states the association more directly and with higher certainty, thereby amplifying the confidence of that specific claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The final random forest model proved reasonably accurate at classifying post ICBT remission with an accuracy of 0.656.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The final random forest model proved reasonably accurate at classifying post ICBT remission with an accuracy of 0.656 and an AUC of 0.687.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the reported accuracy and adds AUC information, increasing the performance detail without changing the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Feature selection retained 45 predictors representing demographic, clinical, process, and genetic data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Feature selection retained 45 predictors representing demographic, clinical, process, and genetic data types.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the feature-selection result verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new, multi-modal classifier for predicting MDD remission status after ICBT treatment was derived and empirically validated.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new, multi-modal classifier for predicting MDD remission status after ICBT treatment was derived and empirically validated.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same contribution claim about deriving and validating a multi-modal classifier with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-modal approach to predicting remission may inform tailored treatment and deserves further investigation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multi-modal approach to predicting remission may inform tailored treatment for major depressive disorder.
alignments.rationale: Both versions convey the same implication that the multi-modal approach could inform tailored treatment; the publication frames it specifically for MDD but does not alter the core suggestion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: D etect IPV can identify Intimate Partner Violence (IPV) from free text with an area under the ROC curve (AUROC) of 89%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DetectIPV can identify intimate partner violence (IPV) from free text with an area under the ROC curve (AUROC) of 89%.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same performance claim (AUROC 89%) for IPV detection from free text; differences are editorial/typographical only.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Physical Abuse can be identified with the greatest accuracy using D etect IPV, achieving an AUROC of 98%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Physical abuse can be identified with the greatest accuracy by DetectIPV, achieving an AUROC of 98%.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-accuracy result (AUROC 98%) for physical abuse detection without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: D etect IPV can identify Emotional Abuse with an AUROC above 80%, although it is the most challenging type to predict.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DetectIPV can identify emotional abuse with an AUROC above 80%, although it is the most challenging type to predict.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same performance characterization for emotional abuse detection (AUROC >80%) and the caveat about difficulty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: D etect IPV is a tool that can be used to reliably detect IPV in various applications, including flagging social media posts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DetectIPV is a reliable tool for detecting IPV in various applications, including flagging social media posts.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about the tool's reliability and applications with no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Digital measures derived from contactless, unobtrusive, and privacy-preserving sensor technologies can be used to build digital clinical outcome assessments relevant to ageing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The digital clinical outcome assessments created from the digital exhaust are powerful and comparable to those obtained from mobile approaches.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core claim that digital measures can produce clinical outcome assessments but frames them as derived from a large digital exhaust and emphasizes comparability to mobile approaches, removing the ageing-specific framing and adding performance context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All digital outcome assessments, except for late-life depression, demonstrated a promising ability (ROC AUC 8 0.7) to discriminate between positive and negative health outcomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The digital clinical outcome assessments created from the digital exhaust are powerful and comparable to those obtained from mobile approaches.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains a claim of strong performance but omits the specific ROC AUC threshold and the exception for late-life depression, thereby revising the quantitative specificity and slightly tempering confidence in the exact reported discrimination metrics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of a digital behaviorome may assist in the discovery of novel potential digital biomarkers for outcomes such as fall risk and mild cognitive impairment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is potential to discover novel digital biomarkers using large-scale remote health monitoring approaches.
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey the same conclusion that large-scale remote/digital behavior data can enable discovery of novel digital biomarkers; the publication restates this potential without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A holistic approach leveraging large sets of digital measures may prove highly beneficial in technology-assisted ageing research.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using connected sensing devices to remotely monitor health can transition healthcare to a more proactive approach.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's recommendation for a holistic digital-measures approach into a broader conclusion about proactive healthcare enabled by connected sensing; this broadens scope from ageing-specific research to healthcare in general.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sensitivity achieved across the sites for TB ranged between 80% - 83% and specificity was to the tune of 92% while using CBNAAT as a reference standard.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sensitivity achieved across the sites for TB ranged between 80% - 83% and specificity was to the tune of 92% while using CBNAAT as a reference standard.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint result verbatim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CXR when used as a reference standard for TB achieved a sensitivity and specificity of 59% and 60% respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CXR when used as a reference standard for TB achieved a sensitivity and specificity of 59% and 60% respectively.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Covid19 achieved a sensitivity & specificity of 92% and 96% while using RT-PCR as the reference standard.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Covid19 achieved a sensitivity & specificity of 92% and 96% while using RT-PCR as the reference standard.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The solution titled 'TimBre' can now be added to the healthcare workers arsenal in situations where a RT-PCR or CXR is not available.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The solution titled 'TimBre' can now be added to the healthcare workers arsenal in situations where a RT-PCR or CXR is not available.
alignments.rationale: Identical conclusion across versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study was primarily focused on the frequency domain that paved way for feature extraction and explainable Machine Learning Models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study was primarily focused on the frequency domain that paved way for feature extraction and explainable Machine Learning Models.
alignments.rationale: Same methodological/contribution claim present in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Methylation risk scores (MRS) significantly improved the imputation of 139 outcomes when added to a baseline set of predictive features.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Methylation risk scores (MRS) significantly improved the imputation of 139 outcomes when added to a baseline set of predictive features.
alignments.rationale: Direct match between preprint and publication result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MRS demonstrated a median improvement of 10.74%, 141.52%, and 15.46% in medications, labs, and diagnosis codes, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MRS demonstrated a median improvement of 10.74%, 141.52%, and 15.46% in medications, labs, and diagnosis codes, respectively.
alignments.rationale: Numerical performance improvements are carried forward identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Including MRS as a medical feature in EHR imputation algorithms significantly improves the imputation in 37% of lab tests examined.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Including MRS as a medical feature in the algorithm significantly improves EHR imputation in 37% of lab tests examined.
alignments.rationale: Same quantified result retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study replicated several MRS in multiple external studies of methylation with a minimum p-value of 2.72 × 10 − 7.
alignments.rationale: Replication result and p-value are preserved; slight reduction in matching confidence due to formatting differences in presentation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work provides a comprehensive evaluation of MRS in comparison to PRS and EHR imputation on the largest dataset consisting of methylation, genotype, and EHR data.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not explicitly state the broader contribution claim about being the most comprehensive evaluation comparing MRS to PRS and EHR on the largest dataset; thus the contribution appears omitted from the published claim list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A younger appearing brain may protect against post stroke neurocognitive disorder (NCD).
alignments.pub_claim_text: A younger appearing brain is associated with a lower risk of post-stroke NCD.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's core finding with equivalent meaning using similar phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher baseline brain age gap (BAG) is associated with higher risk of post stroke NCD at 18 and 36 months.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Higher baseline BAG is associated with higher risk of post-stroke NCD at 18 and 36 months for patients with no NCD at baseline.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that the association pertains to patients without baseline NCD, which clarifies the population for the originally reported association.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Brain age prediction based on brain scans provides a reliable assessment of brain structure.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims focus on BAG associations with NCD and do not include a standalone claim about brain age prediction as a reliable assessment of brain structure, so this contribution appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Brain age prediction is sensitive to post stroke NCD with predictive value for cognitive decline among patients with no impairment at the initial assessment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Higher baseline BAG is associated with higher risk of post-stroke NCD at 18 and 36 months for patients with no NCD at baseline.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the predictive sensitivity specifically as an association of baseline BAG with future NCD in patients without baseline impairment, which clarifies and grounds the preprint's predictive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Continuous Indexing of Fibrosis (CIF) enhances the detection and monitoring of fibrosis within bone marrow trephine samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Continuous Indexing of Fibrosis (CIF) enhances the detection and monitoring of fibrosis within bone marrow trephine samples.
alignments.rationale: The core result about CIF improving fibrosis detection/monitoring is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CIF discriminates between essential thrombocythemia (ET) and pre-fibrotic myelofibrosis (pre-PMF) with high predictive accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CIF, when combined with megakaryocyte feature analysis, discriminates between essential thrombocythemia and pre-fibrotic myelofibrosis with high predictive accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication indicates that discrimination is achieved when CIF is combined with megakaryocyte feature analysis, which revises the preprint's implication that CIF alone provides this discrimination and narrows the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CIF shows significant promise in identifying MPN patients at risk of disease progression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CIF shows promise in identifying MPN patients at risk of disease progression, with predictive features identified in patients diagnosed with essential thrombocythemia.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the promise claim and adds a clarifying detail about predictive features being identified particularly in ET-diagnosed patients.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated analysis of fibrosis can refine disease classification boundaries and inform future studies of micro-environmental factors in MPN.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automated analysis of fibrosis has potential to refine disease classification boundaries and inform future studies of micro-environmental factors in MPN.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same contribution as potential, softening language slightly but keeping the intent and scope intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed machine learning model achieved an area under the ROC curve (AUC) of 0.813 in the test data, indicating its effectiveness in predicting bacteriuria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning model predicting bacteriuria in the emergency department achieved an area under the ROC curve (AUC) of 0.813 in the test data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The wording and numeric result are effectively identical between preprint and publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning model demonstrated higher sensitivity and specificity compared to clinician's judgement for diagnosing urinary tract infections. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning model demonstrated higher sensitivity and specificity compared to proxies of clinician's judgement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same comparative performance claim but clarifies that the comparison was to proxies of clinician judgement rather than direct clinician judgement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Performance of the machine learning model varied with patient characteristics, being lower in patients aged 65 years and older, men, and those with recorded suspicion of UTI. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Performance of the machine learning model varied with patient characteristics, being lower in patients aged 65 years and older and in men. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication omits the subgroup 'those with recorded suspicion of UTI', so the claim is revised and narrowed to the remaining subgroups.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results suggest that tailored models and decision thresholds may be required to account for differences in performance across important patient subgroups. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tailored models and decision thresholds may be required to account for differences in performance across important patient subgroups. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this recommendation verbatim, so the content and implication are unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The results suggest that machine learning could improve diagnosis of suspected urinary tract infections and inform antibiotic prescribing decisions in the emergency department. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This conclusion (explicitly linking ML to improved diagnosis and informing prescribing) appears in the publication but was not stated as a distinct claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The logistic regression model achieved an accuracy of 72%, while the multilayer perceptron and XGBoost models both achieved accuracies of 71%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The logistic regression model achieved an accuracy of 72%, while the multilayer perceptron and XGBoost models both achieved accuracies of 71%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical numeric performance statements between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There was no significant difference in performance between the three models tested, all of which performed better than the external XGBoost model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There was no significant difference in performance between the three models tested, all of which outperformed an external XGBoost model that achieved an accuracy of 63%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same comparative claim but adds the external model's accuracy (63%), clarifying the extent of outperformance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All models showed improved performance with increasing sample size, but limited improvements were observed above a sample size of 400. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: All models showed improved performance with increasing sample size, but limited improvements were observed beyond the current sample size. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's specific '400' threshold to 'current sample size', a minor wording change that slightly reduces specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Improved feature selection, rather than the use of complex models or larger sample sizes alone, is necessary to enhance the prediction of post-operative seizure freedom. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best model performance was achieved with data-driven feature selection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements emphasize that feature selection (data-driven) is key to improved performance, matching the preprint's primary conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified novel predictors for RSV hospitalisation, including esophageal malformations and lower complexity congenital heart defects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Severe congenital heart defects, oesophageal malformations, and lower complexity congenital heart defects are significant predictors for RSV hospital admission. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication lists the same predictors but adds severe congenital heart defects, broadening the set of highlighted predictors beyond the preprint's listing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The clinical prediction model demonstrated a C-statistic of 0.766 in Finland and 0.737 in Sweden, indicating good predictive performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prediction model developed for RSV hospital admission has a C-statistic of 0.766 in Finnish data and 0.737 in Swedish validation data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported C-statistics are identical and the claim is preserved from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Infants in the 90th percentile of predicted RSV hospitalisation probability had 3.3 times higher observed risk than the population’s average. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Infants in the highest decile of predicted RSV hospital admission probability have a 4.5 times higher observed risk compared to others. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a substantially different (larger) observed-risk ratio for the highest decile versus the preprint's figure, so the numeric result was revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Immunisation targeting the top 10% of infants at highest risk would have a number needed to treat of 23 in Finland and 40 in Sweden to prevent hospitalisations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prediction model demonstrates clinical utility in decision curve analysis and hypothetical number needed to treat calculations for immunisation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the clinical-utility conclusion but reframes and generalizes the specific NNT figures into broader decision-curve and hypothetical NNT analyses.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The baseline characteristics and laboratory tests were significantly different between the survivors and nonsurvivors of severe COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The baseline characteristics and laboratory tests were significantly different between survivors and non-survivors of severe COVID-19 infection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding with equivalent wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Four variables (age, high-sensitivity C-reactive protein level, lymphocyte count, and d-dimer level) were selected by all five predictive models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four variables (age, high-sensitivity C-reactive protein level, lymphocyte count, and d-dimer level) were selected by all five predictive models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical selection of predictor variables is reported in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The logistic regression model was selected as the final predictive model due to its simplicity and interpretability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The logistic regression model was selected as the final predictive model due to its simplicity and interpretability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The rationale for selecting logistic regression is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AUROCs of the derivation and external validation sets were 0.895 and 0.881, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AUROCs of the external validation sets were 0.881, with a sensitivity of 0.839 and specificity of 0.794 for the validation set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes external validation performance and adds sensitivity/specificity, while omitting the derivation AUROC reported in the preprint, narrowing the reported scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The individual risk score based on the four selected variables can serve as an index to assess the mortality risk of COVID-19 patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive model can be used to assess the mortality risk of COVID-19 patients based on selected variables. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey the same conclusion that the model/score can be used to assess mortality risk.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transferring an inter-institutional language model between ultrasound and MRI is effective for capturing liver abnormalities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The concept of similar domain adaptation allows for the transfer of inter-institutional language models between ultrasound and MRI to capture liver abnormalities. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same core contribution about cross-modality domain adaptation for liver abnormalities with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The concept of similar domain adaptation improves performance in shared targeted tasks compared to generic language space transfer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transfer learning combined with fine-tuning the discriminative model is more effective for shared targeted tasks than training a language space from scratch. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the mechanism (transfer learning + fine-tuning) and the comparator (training from scratch), making the claim more specific than the preprint's general statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The transfer language space strategy can be applied to automatically label thousands of imaging exams for hepatocellular carcinoma. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The transfer language space strategy achieves an average f1-score greater than 0.9 for automatically labeling imaging exams for hepatocellular carcinoma. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a concrete performance metric (F1 > 0.9) for the automated labeling claim from the preprint, increasing specificity and reported performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Hippocampal-Amygdalo-Ventricular Alzheimer score (HAVAs) shows very accurate detection of patients with Alzheimer's Disease with an AUC ≥ 94%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The innovative MRI-based method for Alzheimer disease detection and mild cognitive impairment prognostic shows very accurate detection of patients with AD compared to control subjects with an AUC of 94% or higher. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same quantitative performance (AUC ≥94%) and scope (AD vs controls) with equivalent certainty and phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method accurately discriminates between progressive mild cognitive impairment (MCI) and stable MCI with an AUC of 78% during a 3 years follow-up. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Hippocampal-Amygdalo-Ventricular Atrophy score (HAVAs) accurately discriminates between progressive MCI and stable MCI with an AUC of 78% during a 3-year follow-up. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same AUC (78%) and follow-up period (3 years) with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method demonstrates better classification performance compared to normative modelling and recent state-of-the-art deep learning methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method demonstrates better classification performance compared to classical machine learning methods and recent state-of-the-art deep learning methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the claim of superior performance versus recent deep learning work but replaces 'normative modelling' with 'classical machine learning', a change in the comparison set (hence a revision) while overall claim and certainty remain.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simplicity of HAVAs makes it fully understandable and well-suited for clinical practice or future pharmaceutical trials. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HAVAs simplicity makes it fully understandable and well-suited for clinical practice or future pharmaceutical trials. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution claim about simplicity and clinical suitability with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Male involvement in maternal health has been conceptualized by focusing on two main aspects: psychosocial support and instrumental support for maternal health care utilization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Male involvement in maternal health has been conceptualised by focusing on two main aspects: psychosocial support and instrumental support for maternal health care utilisation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conceptualisation verbatim, with no substantive change in meaning or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differences in measurement and topics of male involvement in maternal health were noted according to continent. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Differences in measurement and topics of male involvement in maternal health were noted according to continent. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this geographic variation finding directly and with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: One third of studies used one single indicator for assessing male involvement in maternal health, and no common pattern of indicators could be identified. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Researchers often focus on a single aspect of male involvement, resulting in a narrow set of indicators. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same phenomenon qualitatively ('often focus') and as a conclusion about narrow indicators rather than the preprint's quantified 'one third', making the claim less specific (thus clarified/tempered).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A more multidimensional approach to male involvement in maternal health can broaden the potential of MI programs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Researchers often focus on a single aspect of male involvement, resulting in a narrow set of indicators. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the issue as an observed narrowness in indicators; this supports the preprint's recommendation for multidimensional approaches but reframes it as an observed problem rather than an explicit programmatic recommendation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further research is recommended to develop consensus regarding a more robust and comprehensive set of valid and feasible indicators for assessing male involvement in maternal health. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further research is recommended to develop a robust and comprehensive set of indicators for assessing male involvement in maternal health. (certainty=high, type=recommendation)
alignments.rationale: The publication issues essentially the same recommendation for future research and indicator development with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increased social media chatter on Reddit about precipitated opioid withdrawal (POW) correlated with fentanyl mentions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mentions of precipitated opioid withdrawal (POW) on Reddit increased significantly from 2012 to 2021 and were associated with the increase in fentanyl and analog mentions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly links the rise in POW mentions to increases in fentanyl mentions, matching the preprint's correlation claim in time and association.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiences of precipitated opioid withdrawal (POW) were described by individuals who reported sufficient periods of abstinence by standard buprenorphine induction protocols. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individuals reported experiencing precipitated opioid withdrawal (POW) despite prolonged periods of opioid abstinence. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions describe reports of POW occurring despite prolonged abstinence; the publication mirrors the preprint's observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: People with Opioid Use Disorder (OUD) on Reddit are using and recommending microdosing strategies with buprenorphine to avoid POW. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reddit users with Opioid Use Disorder (OUD) are recommending microdosing strategies with buprenorphine to avoid precipitated opioid withdrawal (POW). (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same behavioral observation about user-recommended microdosing strategies with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: People who used fentanyl report experiencing POW following statistically longer periods of abstinence than people who use heroin. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: People who used fentanyl reported experiencing precipitated opioid withdrawal (POW) following statistically longer periods of abstinence than those who used heroin. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative finding regarding fentanyl versus heroin and abstinence durations with matching certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The nnU-Net deep learning framework can be trained out-of-the-box to provide robust fully automatic multi-task segmentation of CT scans performed for computer-assisted orthognathic surgery planning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The nnU-Net deep learning framework can be trained out-of-the-box to provide robust fully automatic multi-task segmentation of CT scans performed for computer-assisted orthognathic surgery planning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this principal conclusion verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The clinical viability of the trained nnU-Net model is shown on a challenging test dataset of 153 CT scans randomly selected from clinical practice, showing metallic artifacts and diverse anatomical deformities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The clinical viability of the trained nnU-Net model is shown on a challenging test dataset of 153 CT scans randomly selected from clinical practice, showing metallic artifacts and diverse anatomical deformities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this test-dataset viability claim identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Commonly used biomedical segmentation evaluation metrics (volumetric and surface Dice Similarity Coefficient) do not always match industry expert evaluation in the case of more demanding clinical applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Commonly used biomedical segmentation evaluation metrics do not always match industry expert evaluation in the case of more demanding clinical applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this cautionary conclusion about metric-expert mismatch with similar emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the test cohort, mean volumetric Dice Similarity Coefficient (vDSC) and surface Dice Similarity Coefficient at 1mm (sDSC) were 0.96 & 0.97 for the upper skull, 0.94 & 0.98 for the mandible, 0.95 & 0.99 for the upper teeth, 0.94 & 0.99 for the lower teeth and 0.82 & 0.98 for the mandibular canal. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the test cohort, the mean volumetric Dice similarity coefficient and surface Dice similarity coefficient for various structures were consistently high, indicating the model's effectiveness. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication summarizes the detailed per-structure numeric results into a general statement of consistently high metrics, removing the specific values (hence clarified and with a narrowed presentation of detail).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Industry expert segmentation approval rates were 93% for the mandible, 89% for the mandibular canal, 82% for the upper skull, 69% for the upper teeth and 58% for the lower teeth. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report these industry expert approval rates; the specific approval-rate results from the preprint are omitted in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Stratipath Breast analysis identified 49.6% of the clinically intermediate tumours as low risk and 50.4% as high risk. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Stratipath Breast analysis identified 49.6% of the clinically intermediate tumors as low risk and 50.4% as high risk. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this exact numeric result with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Prosigna assay classified 32.5%, 47.0% and 20.5% tumours as low, intermediate and high risk, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Prosigna assay classified 32.5%, 47.0% and 20.5% tumors as low, intermediate and high risk, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same Prosigna classification percentages with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall agreement between the Stratipath Breast tool and the Prosigna assay for low-risk and high-risk groups was 71.0%, with a Cohen’s kappa of 0.42. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall agreement between the two tests for low-risk and high-risk groups was 71.0%, with a Cohen’s kappa of 0.42. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the agreement statistics verbatim and with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings demonstrate that image-based risk profiling may aid in the identification of low-risk patients who could potentially be spared adjuvant chemotherapy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results from this clinical evaluation of image-based risk stratification show a considerable agreement to an established gene expression assay in routine breast pathology. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the conclusion as agreement with an established assay rather than explicitly stating that low-risk patients could be spared chemotherapy; this reframes and modestly tempers the original practical implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CTGAN can generate mandibles with different levels and rich morphology, including positional and angular changes and local patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CTGAN can generate mandibles with different levels and rich morphology, including positional and angular changes and local patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim about CTGAN's ability to generate varied mandibular morphology, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 3D generated mandibles have the anatomical morphology of the real mandibles and transition smoothly to the portions without disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 3D generated mandibles have the anatomical morphology of the real mandibles and transition smoothly to the portions without disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about anatomical fidelity and smooth transitions, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CTGAN constructs mandibles with the expected patient characteristics and is suitable for mandibular morphological completion. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CTGAN constructs mandibles with the expected patient characteristics and is suitable for mandibular morphological completion. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about suitability for mandibular completion is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presented modeling principles can be applied to other areas for 3D morphological completion from medical images. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The presented modeling principles can be applied to other areas for 3D morphological completion from medical images. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same claim about generalizability of the modeling principles with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DermoExpert framework achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) of 0.96, 0.95, and 0.97 on the ISIC-2016, ISIC-2017, and ISIC-2018 datasets, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DermoExpert framework achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) of 0.96, 0.95, and 0.97 on the ISIC-2016, ISIC-2017, and ISIC-2018 datasets, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported AUC values are identical in preprint and publication, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results of DermoExpert surpass the recent state-of-the-art by margins of 10.0% and 2.0% for the ISIC-2016 and ISIC-2017 datasets in terms of AUC. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results improve the state-of-the-art by margins of 10.0% and 2.0% for the ISIC-2016 and ISIC-2017 datasets in terms of AUC. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report identical improvement margins over prior work; wording change does not alter the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DermoExpert outperforms by a margin of 3.0% for the ISIC-2018 dataset concerning balanced accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DermoExpert outperforms by 3.0% for the ISIC-2018 dataset concerning balanced accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The balanced-accuracy improvement for ISIC-2018 is reported identically in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework can lead to better recognition of melanoma to assist dermatologists. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DermoExpert provides better classification outcomes on three different datasets, making it a better recognition tool to assist dermatologists. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands wording to reference three datasets and classification outcomes, clarifying the basis for the claim while preserving intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The source code and segmented masks for the ISIC-2018 dataset will be publicly available for further improvements. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The source code and segmented masks for the ISIC-2018 dataset will be available as a public benchmark for future improvements. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the assets as a public benchmark; this is a slight clarification of their intended use but not a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated computational pathology algorithms are effective in screening for adenocarcinoma in various biopsy specimens. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automated computational pathology algorithms are very useful for screening the presence or absence of adenocarcinoma in biopsy specimens. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion about usefulness/effectiveness for screening adenocarcinoma in biopsy specimens with only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multi-organ deep learning models were trained to classify adenocarcinoma in whole slide images of biopsy and radical lymph node dissection specimens. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multi-organ deep learning models were trained to classify adenocarcinoma in biopsy and radical lymph node dissection specimens whole slide images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution describing multi-organ deep learning model training is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models achieved receiver operating characteristic areas under the curves (ROC-AUCs) ranging from 0.91 to 0.99 across seven independent test sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models achieved receiver operating characteristic areas under the curves (ROC-AUCs) in the range of 0.91 - 0.98 across five independent test sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a narrower ROC-AUC range and fewer test sets (0.91–0.98 across five sets vs. 0.91–0.99 across seven), indicating revision and a tempered claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RATING model achieved an accuracy of 86.1% in the clinical trial setting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RATING system outperforms all existing methods, achieving an accuracy of 86.1% on a prospective test dataset and 85.0% on an external test dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication situates the 86.1% figure within prospective/external test contexts and compares to existing methods, which clarifies but does not contradict the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RATING model achieved an accuracy of 85.0% on the US images collected from an external medical center. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RATING system outperforms all existing methods, achieving an accuracy of 86.1% on a prospective test dataset and 85.0% on an external test dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly reports the 85.0% external test accuracy, matching the preprint's external-center result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RATING model improved the average accuracy of ten radiologists from 41.4% to 64.0%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A reader study demonstrates that the RATING system improves the average accuracy of 10 radiologists from 41.4% to 64.0%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reader-study result on radiologists' accuracy is reported identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated AI models for the assessment of RA may facilitate US RA examination and provide support for clinical decision-making. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RATING can indicate possible lesions and enhance diagnostic performance with multimodal ultrasound. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies lesion indication and multimodal ultrasound enhancement, which narrows the general wording into a clearer, modality-specific description while keeping the same conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Resting-state fMRI has the potential to find abnormal behavior in brain activity and to diagnose patients with depression. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Resting-state fMRI has the potential to help doctors detect abnormal behavior in brain activity and to diagnose patients with depression. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's conclusion about resting-state fMRI potential with minimal wording change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Resting-state fMRI has a bias depending on the scanner site, which complicates the diagnosis of depression at new sites. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Resting-state fMRI has a bias depending on the scanner site, which makes it difficult to diagnose depression at a new site. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Both versions convey the same limitation regarding scanner-site bias and its impact on diagnosis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods improve the performance of the diagnosis of major depressive disorder (MDD) at an independent site by reducing site bias effects using regression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose methods to improve the performance of the diagnosis of major depressive disorder (MDD) at an independent site by reducing the site bias effects using regression. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core claim—that methods reduce site bias via regression to improve independent-site diagnosis—is the same, though the publication frames it as a proposed contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification performance of patients with depression is further improved by focusing on melancholic depressive disorder. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific claim about improvement by focusing on melancholic subtype does not appear in the publication's listed claims and thus appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods would be useful to apply depression classifiers to subjects at completely new sites. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Social media can help identify and monitor known and emerging drug epidemics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work suggests that social media can help monitor drug epidemics.
alignments.rationale: The published claim keeps the general monitoring conclusion but omits explicit mention of "identify" and "emerging" epidemics and is framed more tentatively, reducing scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reddit discussions covered a wide variety of drug types that are currently missed by official reporting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We assessed the utility of Reddit data for surveillance of heroin, prescription, and synthetic drugs.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the claim to specific drug categories (heroin, prescription, synthetic) and does not assert that Reddit covers a wide variety missed by official reporting, thus revising and narrowing the original assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The opioid epidemic persists in the United States with over 80,000 deaths annually since 2021, primarily driven by synthetic opioids like fentanyl.
alignments.rationale: No published claim corresponds to the specific epidemiologic statement about annual deaths and fentanyl driving the epidemic; the claim does not appear in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accurate and timely monitoring of the opioid epidemic is needed due to rapidly changing geographic and demographic patterns.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include an explicit claim about the need for timely monitoring or changing geographic/demographic patterns, so this conclusion is not carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Twitter can be a complementary resource for cohort studies of drug safety in pregnancy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings indicate that Twitter can serve as a complementary resource for cohort studies of drug safety in pregnancy.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about Twitter as a complementary resource with similar wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified 2332 tweets from 1195 users that self-report taking a beta-blocker during pregnancy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study retrieved 5114 tweets mentioning a β-blocker, with 2332 tweets self-reporting the use of the medication during pregnancy.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the key figure of 2332 self-reports but adds context (total mentions = 5114), clarifying the dataset rather than changing the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Among 356 pregnancies, we identified 257 during which the beta-blocker was or may have been taken.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study successfully estimated the prenatal time period for 356 pregnancies based on Twitter data.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the ability to estimate prenatal periods for 356 pregnancies; it does not restate the 257-on-treatment count but is clearly referring to the same pregnancy cohort, so the claim is clarified/ reframed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We manually verified an adverse pregnancy outcome for 38 pregnancies, representing 14.8% of those where the beta-blocker was taken.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Among the identified pregnancies, 14.8% experienced adverse outcomes such as preterm birth, NICU admission, low birth weight, birth defects, or miscarriage.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantified adverse outcome rate and describes the outcome types, preserving the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Offering HIV/SRH services in hair salons in Lesotho is largely acceptable and feasible.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Offering HIV/SRH services in hair salons in Lesotho is largely acceptable and feasible.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same overall conclusion about acceptability and feasibility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 93.6% of hair stylists were comfortable offering oral HIV self-testing, 92.4% pre-exposure prophylaxis, and 91.7% post-exposure prophylaxis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 93.6% of hair stylists were comfortable offering oral HIV self-testing, while 93.5% of clients felt comfortable receiving it.
alignments.rationale: The published claim retains the stylist comfort percentage for self-testing but omits the reported PrEP/PEP comfort figures and adds client comfort; this narrows and modifies the original set of percentages.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Immediate demand for HIV self-testing, pre-exposure prophylaxis, and post-exposure prophylaxis was 30.8%, 22.1%, and 14.9%, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the immediate demand percentages presented in the preprint, so this specific result was not carried into the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 90.4% of stylists believed that offering HIV/SRH services would positively impact their business.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 90.4% of stylists believed that offering HIV/SRH services would positively impact their business.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this stylist-perception result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Salons were found to be more accessible than the nearest health facility in terms of cost and time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Salons were found to be more accessible than the nearest health facility in terms of cost and time.
alignments.rationale: The accessibility conclusion is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Qualitative analysis confirmed high acceptability of hair salons as an accessible, less judgmental space than clinics.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a qualitative finding about salons being less judgmental and confirming acceptability that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepFlow is a deep learning system that successfully extracts cardiac flow and volumes from phase contrast cardiac magnetic resonance imaging.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepFlow is a deep-learning system that effectively extracts cardiac flow and volumes from phase-contrast cardiac magnetic resonance imaging.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core contribution of DeepFlow with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A mixed linear model applied to 37,967 individuals from the UK Biobank reveals novel genome-wide significant associations across cardiac dynamic flow volumes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A mixed-linear model applied to 37,653 individuals from the UK Biobank reveals genome-wide significant associations across cardiac dynamic flow volumes.
alignments.rationale: The published claim reports a slightly different sample size and omits the adjective "novel," modestly narrowing the original claim about novelty while preserving the association result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mendelian randomization using CAUSE reveals a causal role for aortic root size in aortic valve regurgitation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mendelian randomization reveals a causal role for aortic root size in aortic valve regurgitation.
