alignments.preprint_claim_text: The diagnostic accuracies for the primary diagnosis were 41.0%, 54.0%, and 33.9% for GPT-4o, Claude 3 Opus, and Gemini 1.5 Pro, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The diagnostic accuracies of GPT-4o, Claude 3 Opus, and Gemini 1.5 Pro for primary diagnosis were 41.0%, 54.0%, and 33.9%, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The numbers and characterization are identical between preprint and publication, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Claude 3 Opus outperformed GPT-4o and Gemini 1.5 Pro in solving radiology quiz cases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Claude 3 Opus outperformed GPT-4o and Gemini 1.5 Pro in solving radiology quiz cases.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is worded the same in both versions with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Significant differences in diagnostic performance were observed among all pairs of the models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Significant differences in the diagnostic performance were observed among all pairs of models.
alignments.rationale: The result sentence is the same in meaning and strength in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The top three differential diagnoses generated by GPT-4o, Claude 3 Opus, and Gemini 1.5 Pro achieved diagnostic accuracies of 49.4%, 62.0%, and 41.0%, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The diagnostic accuracies improved to 49.4%, 62.0%, and 41.0% when considering the accuracy of any of the top three differential diagnoses.
alignments.rationale: Publication reports the same top-three accuracy numbers but frames them as an improvement relative to primary diagnosis, adding contextual clarification.

alignments.pub_claim_text: These models appear capable of assisting radiologists when supplied with accurate evaluations and worded descriptions of imaging findings.
alignments.rationale: This interpretive conclusion about assistive potential is not present in the listed preprint claims and appears as a new high-level conclusion in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine learning-based stratification system was developed to identify patients at risk of high blood glucose levels in gestational diabetes mellitus.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning-based stratification system can identify patients at risk of high blood glucose levels based on daily measurements and EHR data.
alignments.rationale: Publication adds detail on inputs (daily measurements and EHR data) while preserving the original contribution claim, so it is a clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The XGBoost model achieved the highest performance during internal validation with specific metrics for mean squared error, R2, and mean absolute error.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The XGBoost model achieved the highest performance during internal validation with specific metrics for MSE, R2, and MAE.
alignments.rationale: The reported model performance and metrics are the same between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrated similar performance during external validation, indicating its generalizability across different cohorts of GDM patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model performed similarly during external validation, indicating its generalizability across different cohorts of GDM patients.
alignments.rationale: The external validation and generalizability claim is worded consistently in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ML-assisted genome-wide association studies (GWAS) are at risk for false positive associations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ML-assisted genome-wide association studies (GWAS) are at risk for false-positive associations.
alignments.rationale: Same cautionary result is carried forward verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: POP-GWAS is a novel statistical framework that provides valid statistical inference regardless of imputation quality or the algorithms used.
alignments.pub_claim_text: POP-GWAS ensures valid and powerful statistical inference irrespective of imputation quality and choice of algorithm.
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the inference validity claim with added emphasis on power, clarifying the guarantee and amplifying confidence in the method's performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using POP-GWAS, we identified 89 novel loci associated with bone mineral density (BMD) that reached genome-wide significance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using POP-GWAS, 89 new loci were identified for bone mineral density, revealing skeletal site-specific genetic architecture.
alignments.rationale: The novel loci finding and its association with BMD is preserved; publication combines it with the architecture observation but the loci claim itself is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of POP-GWAS reveals a skeletal site-specific genetic architecture of bone mineral density.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using POP-GWAS, 89 new loci were identified for bone mineral density, revealing skeletal site-specific genetic architecture.
alignments.rationale: The skeletal site-specific architecture finding appears in the publication alongside the novel loci; the substantive result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework of POP-GWAS may fundamentally reshape analytical strategies in future ML-assisted GWAS.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Post-Prediction GWAS (POP-GWAS) is a statistical framework that redesigns GWAS on ML-imputed outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's speculative language about reshaping strategies is rendered in the publication as a more concrete statement that the framework 'redesigns' GWAS; this both clarifies and narrows the claim while amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantile IV achieves low estimation error in a wide range of plausible MR scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantile IV achieves a low estimation error in a wide range of plausible MR scenarios.
alignments.rationale: Identical methodological performance claim across versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A genetically predicted reduction in sclerostin levels significantly increases heel bone mineral density and reduces the risk of osteoporosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A genetically predicted reduction in sclerostin levels significantly increases heel bone mineral density and reduces the risk of osteoporosis.
alignments.rationale: The causal result and its interpretation are consistent between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is no discernible effect of circulating sclerostin levels on ischemic cardiovascular diseases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is no discernible effect of genetically predicted reduction in sclerostin levels on ischemic cardiovascular diseases.
alignments.rationale: The exposure definition shifts from measured circulating sclerostin to genetically predicted reduction; this is a substantive revision that narrows the claim to the genetic instrumented exposure.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantile IV offers a flexible approach for subgroup analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantile IV contributes to the advancement of MR methodology.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized subgroup analysis flexibility; the publication generalizes the methodological contribution to MR more broadly, which broadens the scope of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel adaptive DBS (aDBS) algorithm demonstrates enhanced clinical efficacy compared to conventional DBS and a control algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants reported increased therapeutic efficacy with aDBS compared to conventional DBS (cDBS).
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the general efficacy claim with specific patient-reported outcomes and comparison to cDBS, narrowing the original broad efficacy statement into measured patient-reported effects.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The aDBS algorithm leads to improvements in movement speed, reduced dyskinesia, and better patient-reported therapeutic quality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The aDBS approach resulted in increased typing speed and reduced dyskinesia compared to cDBS.
alignments.rationale: Publication preserves the movement speed and dyskinesia aspects but specifies typing speed and comparison to cDBS, making the claim more specific.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine learning pipeline capable of remotely optimizing aDBS parameters in a home setting has been demonstrated.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning pipeline was demonstrated to remotely optimize aDBS parameters in a home setting.
alignments.rationale: The pipeline demonstration is described the same way in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Movement-responsive aDBS has the potential to serve as a new therapeutic approach for advanced Parkinson's Disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work illustrates the potential of movement-responsive aDBS as a promising therapeutic approach.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication make the same cautious claim about therapeutic potential.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The adaptive DBS (aDBS) algorithm demonstrated improvements in dominant hand movement speeds compared to an inverted control.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a specific result about dominant hand movement speed versus an inverted control that is not explicitly present in the listed preprint claims, so it is treated as an added, more granular result.
